source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: FIA REX vt. 7°42; 带来 (of an action or process) produce or deliver (a result or gain): This method yields the same results. 这一方法带来了同样的结果 。 近义词 engender Vv.i， 届服; 让步 give way to arguments, demands, or pressure: The company refused to yield to the protesters' demands. 公司拒绝向抗议者让步 。 #3444) succumb n. (工业或农产品的 ) 产量; 产出 the full amount of an agricultural or industrial product: yield of wheat per acre 每英亩小麦产量近义词 output 派生 unyielding /An'jildm/ adj. 不屈服的; 坚定的; 顽强的 (of a person or their behavior) unlikely to be swayed; resolute: unyielding determination 坚定不移的决心近义词 implacable

headline.core_en: to produce a result, give way to pressure, or refer to an amount produced

familiar_bridge.summary: produce 是“产生”；yield 更常用于方法、过程或土地“产出结果”，也可表示人在压力下让步。

core.main: yield 是多义词：方法可以 yield results，个人或机构可以 yield to pressure，农田或产业有 yield。三者都围绕“给出/让出某种结果”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某个方法、行动或过程带来结果、收益或数据。

examples.sentence: This method yields the same results in repeated trials.
examples.translation: 这一方法在重复试验中产生相同结果。
examples.teaching_note: yield results 是这个用法。

examples.sentence: The survey yielded little evidence of public support.
examples.translation: 调查几乎没有产生公众支持的证据。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境里 yield evidence 很自然。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 面对论点、要求或压力时退让。

examples.sentence: The company refused to yield to the protesters' demands.
examples.translation: 公司拒绝向抗议者的要求让步。
examples.teaching_note: yield to demands 是让步义。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 农作物、工业或投资产生的数量。

examples.sentence: The new seed increased the yield of wheat per acre.
examples.translation: 新品种提高了每英亩小麦产量。
examples.teaching_note: yield 作名词表示产量。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The process produced reliable results.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: produce 最普通；yield 更常用于结果、数据、收益自然产出。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mayor refused to give in to pressure.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: give in 更口语；yield 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy engendered public distrust.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: engender 强调引发某种状态或情绪。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The government succumbed to public pressure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: succumb 更强调被压力压倒。

antonyms.example_sentence: The group resisted pressure to change its position.
antonyms.difference_note: resist 与 yield to pressure 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The agency withheld the data.
antonyms.difference_note: withhold 与 yield/produce information 的交出或产出相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Factory output rose in June.
confusable_words.difference_note: output 是产出量；yield 可指产量，也可作动词。

word_family.example_sentence: The yielding surface reduced the force of the fall.
Her unyielding stance frustrated the negotiators.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 yield 的名词义。
related_words.example_sentence: The factory increased its output this year.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (averse to) 厌恶的; 反感的; 反对的 having a strong dislike of or opposition to something: He is averse to flattery. {h F EK KEE. 3K aversion /a'vaifan/ n. 厌恶; 反感 a strong dislike or disinclination: have an aversion to war 对战争感到厌恶

headline.core_en: strongly disliking or opposed to something

familiar_bridge.summary: dislike 是“不喜欢”；averse 更正式，常写作 be averse to，表示心理上排斥或不愿接受。

core.main: averse 后面几乎总跟 to：be averse to flattery / risk / change。它不是普通不喜欢，而是有明显抵触。

usage_groups.group_title: 对某事反感或不愿接受
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 心理上排斥某种行为、风险、变化或奉承。

examples.sentence: He is averse to flattery and distrusts excessive praise.
examples.translation: 他反感奉承，也不信任过度赞美。
examples.teaching_note: be averse to flattery 对应原书。

examples.sentence: Investors who are averse to risk may prefer safer assets.
examples.translation: 厌恶风险的投资者可能更喜欢安全资产。
examples.teaching_note: risk-averse 是非常常见的相关表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She was unwilling to accept the offer.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unwilling 是不愿做；averse 带更强反感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Several members were opposed to the plan.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: opposed 更偏立场反对。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He was disinclined to discuss the matter publicly.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disinclined 比 unwilling 更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic was antipathetic to sentimental art.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: antipathetic 更强，强调明显反感。

antonyms.example_sentence: She was willing to revise the proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: willing 是直接反向。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee was receptive to new ideas.
antonyms.difference_note: receptive 强调愿意接纳。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The drug had adverse effects.
confusable_words.difference_note: adverse 是“不利的”；averse 是“反感的”，常和 to 连用。

word_family.note: 名词，常见 an aversion to。
word_family.example_sentence: She has an aversion to unnecessary risk.
Risk-averse investors avoided the volatile stock.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. (shy from 或 shy away from) 躲避; 回避 to try to avoid (something) because of nervousness, fear, dislike, etc.: an author who shies away from publicity 回避媒体关注的作家变形 shies, shying, shied

headline.core_en: to avoid something because of fear or dislike; also timid or reserved

familiar_bridge.summary: avoid 是“避开”；shy away from 更像因为害怕、紧张或不喜欢而退缩。

core.main: 本页重点是动词短语 shy away from：人或机构回避曝光、风险、责任或争议。形容词 shy 是基础义。

usage_groups.group_title: 因为紧张或不愿意而回避
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 面对公众、风险、责任或困难话题时退缩、避开。

examples.sentence: The author shies away from publicity and rarely gives interviews.
examples.translation: 这位作家回避媒体关注，很少接受采访。
examples.teaching_note: shy away from publicity 对应原书。

examples.sentence: The committee should not shy away from difficult questions.
examples.translation: 委员会不应回避困难问题。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于抽象争议或责任。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 性格上不善于和人接触，容易紧张。

examples.sentence: He was shy around strangers but confident with close friends.
examples.translation: 他在陌生人面前害羞，但和密友在一起很自信。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词义是基础义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speaker avoided the most controversial issue.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: avoid 最普通；shy away from 带退缩或不愿面对的感觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The timid child spoke softly.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: timid 对应形容词 shy 的性格义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official evaded the question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: evade 常暗示有意逃避责任或问题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She recoiled from the idea of public debate.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: recoil 更强，带身体或心理后退感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report confronts the problem directly.
antonyms.difference_note: confront 与 shy away from 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her outgoing personality made interviews easy.
antonyms.difference_note: outgoing 与形容词 shy 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The total was just shy of one million dollars.
confusable_words.difference_note: just shy of 是“差一点到”，不是害羞或回避。

word_family.example_sentence: Her shyness made the first interview difficult.
The company kept shying away from public questions.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 shy away from。
related_words.example_sentence: Avoidance can make a small problem worse.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 非常宝贵的; 极为贵重的; 无价的 extremely useful: Good health is an invaluable blessing. 身体健康是千金难买的好福气 。 | Thank you for your invaluable help. 谢谢你非常宝贵的帮助

headline.core_cn: 极有价值的；非常宝贵的
headline.core_en: extremely useful or valuable

familiar_bridge.summary: valuable 是“有价值的”；invaluable 不是“没有价值”，而是“价值大到难以估量”。

core.main: invaluable 常形容 help、advice、experience、evidence、resource，语气很正面。

usage_groups.group_title: 帮助、建议或资源极其有用
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某物非常有帮助、价值难以估量。

examples.sentence: Thank you for your invaluable help during the project.
examples.translation: 谢谢你在项目中提供的宝贵帮助。
examples.teaching_note: invaluable help 对应原书。

examples.sentence: Her experience proved invaluable when the team faced a crisis.
examples.translation: 团队遇到危机时，她的经验证明极其宝贵。
examples.teaching_note: prove invaluable 是自然结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 健康、机会等珍贵到难以用钱衡量
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某种东西非常珍贵，不只是价格高。

examples.sentence: Good health is an invaluable blessing.
examples.translation: 健康是千金难买的福气。
examples.teaching_note: 对应

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The advice was valuable to new teachers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: valuable 表示有价值；invaluable 表示价值高到难以估量。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The guide was useful during the trip.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: useful 只说有用；invaluable 强调非常有用。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Accurate data are indispensable to the study.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indispensable 强调少了不行。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The letter is a priceless record of the period.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: priceless 更偏珍贵或情感价值。

antonyms.example_sentence: The forged document was worthless as evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: worthless 与 invaluable 的高价值相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The broken tool was useless.
antonyms.difference_note: useless 与 extremely useful 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The rumor was valueless to the investigation.
confusable_words.difference_note: invaluable 容易误解成“无价值”，但实际是“极有价值”。

extension.root_affix: in- 在 invaluable 中不是普通否定，而是加强为“价值难以衡量”。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: The archive has great historical value.
The data were valuable to researchers.
The valuation of the property took several days.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt. AIA; 否认有 …; 和否认与 … 有关 refuse to acknowledge or maintain any connection with: disown a statement 否认某名话是自己说的 | He disowned any hand in the book. 他否认曾插手该书的编写工作

headline.core_en: to refuse to acknowledge a connection with someone or something

familiar_bridge.summary: own 是“承认是自己的”；disown 就是“不认”，可以是否认话、行为、责任，也可以和人断绝关系。

core.main: disown 常用于 disown a statement / disown responsibility / disown a child。重点是拒绝承认关系或责任。

usage_groups.group_title: 否认某话、行为或责任和自己有关
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 拒绝承认某个说法、行为、责任出自自己。

examples.sentence: The minister quickly disowned the statement after it caused public anger.
examples.translation: 这句话引发公众愤怒后，部长很快否认它代表自己。

examples.sentence: He disowned any hand in the book.
examples.translation: 他否认曾参与这本书的编写。
examples.teaching_note: disown any hand in something 是原书结构。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 正式或情感上不再承认某人为家庭、团体或阵营的一员。

examples.sentence: The family disowned him after the scandal.
examples.translation: 丑闻之后，家人和他断绝关系。
examples.teaching_note: 人际关系中 disown 语气很重。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She denied making the comment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: deny 更普通；disown 强调不承认关系或责任。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee rejected the proposal.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reject 是拒绝接受；disown 是不认作自己的。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader repudiated the agreement.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: repudiate 更正式，常用于原则、协议或责任。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He renounced his claim to the property.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renounce 强调主动宣布放弃。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author acknowledged his role in the project.
antonyms.difference_note: acknowledge 与 disown 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Several groups claimed responsibility for the attack.
antonyms.difference_note: claim responsibility 与 disown responsibility 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The organization disavowed the violent statement.
confusable_words.difference_note: disavow 与 disown 接近，更正式，常用于公开声明。

extension.root_affix: dis- 表示分离或否定；own 是“拥有/承认属于自己”。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: She owned the mistake and apologized.

word_family.meaning: 被断绝关系的；被否认的
word_family.example_sentence: The disowned statement disappeared from the website.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The party disavowed the candidate's remarks.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. Rit; (一系列的 ) 祖先 a line of descent traced continuously from an ancestor: They've been researching their genealogies. 他们在研究自己的家谱

headline.core_en: a line of family descent, or the study of that descent

familiar_bridge.summary: family tree 是“家谱”；genealogy 更正式，可以指家族血统的研究，也可比喻思想、概念的来源谱系。

core.main: genealogy 既可用于人的祖先和家谱，也可在学术语境中说某个观念或制度的历史来源。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 追溯一个家族从祖先延续下来的血统线。

examples.sentence: They have been researching their genealogies for years.
examples.translation: 他们多年来一直在研究自己的家谱。
examples.teaching_note: 对应

examples.sentence: The archive helped her trace her genealogy back three centuries.
examples.translation: 档案帮助她把家谱追溯到三百年前。
examples.teaching_note: trace genealogy 是自然结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 观念、制度或传统的来源谱系
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在学术写作中，比喻追溯一个观念、制度或文化形式的历史来源。

examples.sentence: The book traces the genealogy of modern environmental thought.
examples.translation: 这本书追溯了现代环保思想的谱系。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境中 genealogy 可比喻思想来源。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The family tree shows four generations.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: family tree 更日常、图像化；genealogy 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The family claims a royal lineage.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lineage 强调一条血统线。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She studied her ancestry through old records.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ancestry 更强调祖先来源。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The tradition is passed down through descent.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: descent 是从祖先延续下来的线。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Genetics explains how traits are inherited.
confusable_words.difference_note: genetics 是生物遗传机制；genealogy 是家族谱系或来源追溯。

extension.root_affix: genea- 与 birth/family lineage 有关；-logy 表示研究。

word_family.example_sentence: Genealogical records helped identify the family.
A genealogist found the missing branch of the family.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 genealogy 的家族线索。
related_words.example_sentence: The lineage can be traced through written records.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 失态; 失礼; AZAR an unintentional act or remark causing embarrassment to its originator; a blunder: an unforgivable social gaffe 不可侥想的社交失礼

headline.core_en: an embarrassing mistake in speech or behavior, usually unintentional

familiar_bridge.summary: mistake 是“错误”；gaffe 更具体，是公开场合或社交场合里让人尴尬的失言、失礼。

core.main: gaffe 常形容 politician、speaker、guest 的一句话或一个举动造成尴尬。它通常不是故意冒犯。

usage_groups.group_title: 公开或社交场合的尴尬失误
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 无意的一句话或行为让说话者自己尴尬、失礼。

examples.sentence: The candidate committed a political gaffe during the interview.
examples.translation: 候选人在采访中犯了一个政治失言。
examples.teaching_note: political gaffe 很常见。

examples.sentence: Forgetting the host's name was an unforgivable social gaffe.
examples.translation: 忘记主人的名字是一次不可原谅的社交失礼。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The error was a simple mistake.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mistake 范围很宽；gaffe 特指尴尬的社交或公开失误。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision was a costly blunder.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: blunder 可是严重错误，不一定社交尴尬。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Asking about her salary was a faux pas.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: faux pas 更偏礼仪失误。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The letter revealed a youthful indiscretion.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indiscretion 强调轻率或判断失误。

antonyms.example_sentence: She handled the question with tact.
antonyms.difference_note: tact 是避免 gaffe 的社交分寸。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The fisherman used a gaff to lift the fish.
confusable_words.difference_note: gaff 是工具；gaffe 是失言/失礼。

word_family.note: 复合形容词，常用于政治人物。
word_family.example_sentence: The gaffe-prone official avoided live interviews.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 不明智的; 欠考虑的 resulting from or showing lack of wise and sufficient counsel or deliberation: an ill-advised decision 不明智的决定 i034] misguided

headline.core_en: not sensible because it was done without enough thought or good advice

familiar_bridge.summary: unwise 是“不明智”；ill-advised 更像一个决定或行动在做之前就缺少充分考虑。

core.main: ill-advised 常形容 decision、move、remark、policy，语气是“这事本来就不该这么做”。

usage_groups.group_title: 决定、行动或评论欠考虑
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 行动前缺少足够判断、建议或审慎思考，因此显得不明智。

examples.sentence: The mayor made an ill-advised decision to ignore the warnings.
examples.translation: 市长无视警告的决定很不明智。

examples.sentence: His ill-advised remarks damaged the company's reputation.
examples.translation: 他欠考虑的言论损害了公司的声誉。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于公开言论。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was unwise to ignore the evidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unwise 最普通；ill-advised 强调缺少考虑或建议。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan seemed foolish from the start.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: foolish 语气更直接、更口语。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: It was imprudent to invest all the funds at once.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: imprudent 更正式，强调缺少谨慎。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform was based on a misguided assumption.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: misguided 强调方向或判断被错误想法带偏。

antonyms.example_sentence: A prudent investor keeps some money in reserve.
antonyms.difference_note: prudent 与 ill-advised 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The delay was a well-advised precaution.
antonyms.difference_note: well-advised 是结构上的直接对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The ill-mannered guest interrupted everyone.
confusable_words.difference_note: ill-mannered 是礼貌问题；ill-advised 是判断问题。

extension.root_affix: ill- 在复合词中表示“不好、糟糕”；advised 来自 advice。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: Good advice can prevent an ill-advised choice.

word_family.note: 常用于 it is advisable to...
word_family.example_sentence: It is advisable to read the contract carefully.
Her cautious response was well-advised.

source.raw_ocr_text: aE v.i.&v.t. 收缩; 缩小 decrease in size, number, or range: The muscle contracts. 肌肉收缩 。 3 044) dwindle v.t. RY ( 疾病等 ) catch or develop (a disease or infectious agent): contract a virus 感染病毒

headline.core_en: to become smaller, catch a disease, or make a formal agreement

familiar_bridge.summary: contract 作为动词有三个常见画面：肌肉“收缩”，人“感染”疾病，双方“订立合同”。要靠宾语判断。

core.main: 本页原书重点是收缩和感染：muscle contracts；contract a virus。合同义也常见，但要和动词重音区别开来。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 大小、数量、范围或肌肉变小、变紧。

examples.sentence: The muscle contracts when the nerve sends a signal.
examples.translation: 神经发出信号时，肌肉会收缩。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书 The muscle contracts。

examples.sentence: The economy contracted during the crisis.
examples.translation: 危机期间经济萎缩。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象范围或规模也可 contract。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人染上病毒、疾病或感染。

examples.sentence: Several workers contracted the virus during the outbreak.
examples.translation: 疫情期间几名工人感染了病毒。
examples.teaching_note: contract a virus 对应原书。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 正式达成合同关系。

examples.sentence: The city contracted with a private firm to repair the bridge.
examples.translation: 市政府与一家私营公司签约修桥。
examples.teaching_note: 这是合同义，和名词 contract 相关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fabric shrank after washing.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: shrink 是日常“缩小”；contract 更正式，可用于肌肉、经济。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She caught a cold at school.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: catch a cold 更口语；contract a disease 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Their savings dwindled over the winter.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dwindle 强调数量逐渐减少。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Cold air can constrict blood vessels.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: constrict 强调变窄或被勒紧。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company expanded into new markets.
antonyms.difference_note: expand 与 contract 的缩小义相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The pupils dilated in the dark.
antonyms.difference_note: dilate 常用于瞳孔、血管等扩张。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The contract required both parties to share costs.
confusable_words.difference_note: 名词重音在前；动词重音通常在后。

extension.root_affix: con- 有“共同”联想，tract 有“拉”的词源感；但 contract 的现代义要按搭配判断。

word_family.example_sentence: Muscle contraction requires energy.
The dispute involved contractual obligations.

word_family.note: 签合同承担工作的人或机构。
word_family.example_sentence: The contractor repaired the roof.

related_words.note: 同样有收紧变窄的感觉。
related_words.example_sentence: The bandage can constrict blood flow.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 遗产 an amount of money or property left to someone in a will: a substantial legacy 数量可观的遗产

headline.core_en: money or property left after death, or something handed down from the past

familiar_bridge.summary: inheritance 是“继承来的遗产”；legacy 还常指一个人、制度或时代留下的长远影响。

core.main: legacy 有两个核心方向：法律/财产上的遗赠，以及历史、政治、文化上的遗留影响。

usage_groups.group_title: 遗嘱中留下的钱或财产
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某人去世后留给他人的金钱或财产。

examples.sentence: She received a substantial legacy from her aunt.
examples.translation: 她从姑妈那里继承了一大笔遗产。
examples.teaching_note: substantial legacy 对应原书。

usage_groups.group_title: 过去留下的影响或后果
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人、政策、战争或时代留下的长期影响，可以正面也可以负面。

examples.sentence: The leader left a mixed legacy of reform and repression.
examples.translation: 这位领导者留下了改革与压制并存的复杂遗产。
examples.teaching_note: legacy 常用于评价历史人物。

examples.sentence: The city still struggles with the legacy of industrial pollution.
examples.translation: 这座城市仍在应对工业污染留下的后果。
examples.teaching_note: legacy of 常指历史遗留问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The house became part of her inheritance.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: inheritance 更偏财产；legacy 还可指历史影响。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The festival celebrates the region's cultural heritage.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: heritage 多指文化传统或共同遗产。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum received a bequest from a collector.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bequest 是法律/遗嘱中的遗赠，更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The monuments are part of the nation's patrimony.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: patrimony 更书面，常指民族或家族继承物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The story became a local legend.
confusable_words.difference_note: legend 是传说或传奇；legacy 是遗留物或影响。

word_family.note: 法律词，指遗嘱中的受遗赠者。
word_family.example_sentence: The legatee received the painting after the will was read.

related_words.note: 帮助理解财产义。
related_words.example_sentence: The inheritance was divided among the children.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ”哺乳动物 a warm-blooded vertebrate animal of a class that is distinguished by the possession of hair or fur, the secretion of milk by females for the nourishment of the young, and (typically) the birth of live young: Tiger is a kind of mammal. 老虎是一种哺乳动物

headline.core_en: a warm-blooded animal whose females produce milk for their young

familiar_bridge.summary: animal 是“动物”；mammal 是动物分类中的“哺乳动物”，如 tiger、whale、human。

core.main: mammal 是科学分类词，强调温血、有毛或皮毛、雌性用乳汁哺育幼崽。

usage_groups.group_title: 动物分类中的哺乳动物
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指具有哺乳特征的一类动物。

examples.sentence: A tiger is a kind of mammal.
examples.translation: 老虎是一种哺乳动物。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书例句。

examples.sentence: Whales are marine mammals, not fish.
examples.translation: 鲸是海洋哺乳动物，不是鱼。
examples.teaching_note: 帮助区分 mammal 和 fish。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The animal left tracks in the snow.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: animal 是大类；mammal 是其中一类。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The forest is home to many small creatures.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: creature 更泛，常用于未知或一般生物。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Birds and mammals are vertebrates.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vertebrate 按有无脊椎分类，不等于 mammal。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A lizard is a reptile, not a mammal.
confusable_words.difference_note: reptile 是另一类动物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A frog is an amphibian.
confusable_words.difference_note: amphibian 不是 mammal。

word_family.example_sentence: Mammalian species feed their young with milk.

related_words.note: 科学分类中的上位概念。
related_words.example_sentence: Most mammals are vertebrates with hair or fur.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 令人遗憾的; 令人失望的; 令人难过的 (of conduct or an event) giving rise to regret; undesirable; unwelcome: a regrettable mistake 邻人和遗憾的错误

headline.core_en: unfortunate and causing regret

familiar_bridge.summary: regret 是“后悔/遗憾”；regrettable 是事情本身让人觉得遗憾。

core.main: regrettable 常形容 mistake、incident、decision、delay。它比 bad 更克制，常用于正式说明或批评。

usage_groups.group_title: 错误、事件或决定令人遗憾
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某件事不理想，让人觉得本不该发生。

examples.sentence: The delay was regrettable, but it did not ruin the project.
examples.translation: 这次延误令人遗憾，但没有毁掉项目。
examples.teaching_note: regrettable 常用于正式、克制的评价。

examples.sentence: The judge called the error a regrettable mistake.
examples.translation: 法官称这个错误令人遗憾。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The timing of the announcement was unfortunate.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unfortunate 更常用；regrettable 更强调让人遗憾。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was sad to see the old theater close.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sad 更情绪化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report described the neglect as lamentable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lamentable 更正式、更强烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conditions in the prison were deplorable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deplorable 带道德谴责。

antonyms.example_sentence: It was fortunate that no one was hurt.
antonyms.difference_note: fortunate 与 regrettable 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The improvement was a welcome surprise.
antonyms.difference_note: welcome 表示正面、令人高兴。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She sounded regretful after the argument.
confusable_words.difference_note: regretful 形容人；regrettable 形容事。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: He expressed regret for the mistake.
Her regretful tone softened the apology.
Regrettably, the evidence was incomplete.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 回避;， 避开 (问题 、 人等 ) avoid dealing with or discussing (something problematic or disagreeable): sidestep a question 回避问题 | sidestep a responsibility 逃避责任 3 44] circumvent

headline.core_en: to avoid dealing with a difficult question, issue, or responsibility

familiar_bridge.summary: avoid 是“避开”；sidestep 更像“侧身绕开”，常用于回避问题或责任。

core.main: sidestep 常接 question、issue、responsibility、controversy，带有不正面回应的感觉。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不直接处理棘手问题，而是绕开。

examples.sentence: The minister sidestepped the question about campaign funding.
examples.translation: 部长回避了有关竞选资金的问题。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配很常见。

examples.sentence: The company tried to sidestep responsibility for the accident.
examples.translation: 公司试图回避事故责任。
examples.teaching_note: sidestep responsibility 很自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speaker avoided the sensitive topic.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: avoid 更普通；sidestep 暗示绕开本该面对的问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The official dodged every difficult question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: dodge 更口语，常带批评。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The firm circumvented the regulation through a loophole.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: circumvent 更正式，常指绕过规则。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness evaded the question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: evade 常暗示故意逃避。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report confronts the problem directly.
antonyms.difference_note: confront 与 sidestep 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor addressed the complaint in detail.
antonyms.difference_note: address 强调正面处理。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The children walked on the sidewalk.
confusable_words.difference_note: sidewalk 是名词；sidestep 是动词。

word_family.example_sentence: His answer was a careful sidestep.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The plan circumvented the usual approval process.

headline.core_en: a public sale in which goods are sold to the highest bidder

familiar_bridge.summary: sale 是“出售”；auction 是多人出价，价高者得。

core.main: auction 可以是名词“拍卖会/拍卖”，也可作动词“拍卖某物”。核心是竞价。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: auction 可以是名词“拍卖会/拍卖”，也可作动词“拍卖某物”。核心是竞价。

examples.sentence: The museum sold the painting by auction.
examples.translation: 博物馆通过拍卖出售了这幅画。

examples.sentence: The family auctioned off the old farm equipment.
examples.translation: 这家人拍卖掉了旧农具。
examples.teaching_note: auction off 是常见动词结构。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The sale attracted many buyers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sale 泛指出售；auction 强调竞价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Several collectors bid on the painting.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bid 是拍卖中的出价行为。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The estate was offered at a public sale.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: public sale 更正式，auction 是具体竞价形式。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She attended an audition for the play.
confusable_words.difference_note: audition 形近音近，但意思完全不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The auctioneer opened the bidding at five hundred dollars.
The highest bidder won the antique chair.
Her final bid surprised the room.

headline.core_en: old-fashioned and no longer suitable for modern use

familiar_bridge.summary: old 是“旧”；antiquated 更强调旧到不合时宜。

core.main: antiquated 常形容 equipment、custom、law、system，通常带批评意味。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某物已经老旧到不适合当前需要，通常带负面评价。

examples.sentence: The school still uses antiquated equipment in its science lab.

examples.teaching_note: antiquated equipment 对应原书。

examples.sentence: The village abandoned an antiquated custom that excluded outsiders.
examples.translation: 村庄废除了一项排斥外人的陈旧习俗。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于习俗和制度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The old-fashioned rule frustrated younger workers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: old-fashioned 较普通；antiquated 更强调过时到不适用。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The software is outdated and insecure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: outdated 常指信息、技术或工具过时。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The device became obsolete within a decade.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obsolete 比 antiquated 更强调已被淘汰。

antonyms.example_sentence: The hospital uses modern equipment.
antonyms.difference_note: modern 与 antiquated 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She bought an antique mirror at the market.
confusable_words.difference_note: antique 可有收藏价值；antiquated 通常是负面。

word_family.note: 相关词，但语气可能正面。
word_family.example_sentence: The shop sells antique furniture.
The museum studies objects from antiquity.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 'il; 专利权 a government authority or license conferring a right or title for a set period, especially the sole right to exclude others from making, using, or selling an invention: take out a patent for an invention 取得一项发明的专利 vit. 为 … 获得专利权; 对 … 子以专利保护 obtain a patent for (an invention): They patented their invention. 他们为自己的发明申请了专利

headline.core_en: a legal right to control the making, use, or sale of an invention

familiar_bridge.summary: right 是“权利”；patent 是政府授予发明者的排他性权利。

core.main: patent 可作名词“专利”，也可作动词“申请/获得专利保护”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: patent 可作名词“专利”，也可作动词“申请/获得专利保护”。

examples.sentence: They patented their invention before showing it to investors.
examples.translation: 他们在向投资者展示发明前先申请了专利。

examples.sentence: The company took out a patent for the new device.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Copyright protects original works.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: copyright 保护作品；patent 保护发明。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The name was registered as a trademark.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: trademark 保护品牌标识。

antonyms.example_sentence: The design eventually entered the public domain.
antonyms.difference_note: public domain 表示不再受专利/版权等排他权保护。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The claim was patently false.
confusable_words.difference_note: patently 是副词“显然地”，和专利义不同。

related_words.note: 专利通常保护发明。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 内容空洞的; 没有思想的 having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence; mindless: a vacuous remark % #4 iE

headline.core_en: empty of meaningful thought or intelligence

familiar_bridge.summary: empty 是“空的”；vacuous 用来批评话语、表情或观点空洞无物。

core.main: vacuous 常形容 remark、speech、smile、slogan，强调没有实质内容。

usage_groups.group_title: 话语或观点空洞无思想
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调话语、表情或观点看似存在，却没有真实思想或内容。

examples.sentence: The candidate offered only vacuous slogans instead of a real plan.
examples.translation: 候选人只提出空洞口号，没有真正计划。
examples.teaching_note: vacuous slogans 是自然批评。

examples.sentence: Her vacuous smile made the apology seem insincere.
examples.translation: 她空洞的微笑让道歉显得不真诚。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容表情空洞。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech was empty despite its confident tone.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: empty 更普通；vacuous 更强调缺少思想。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The review dismissed the film as mindless entertainment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mindless 更口语，强调不用脑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The host asked several inane questions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inane 带愚蠢感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The proposal included substantive reforms.
antonyms.difference_note: substantive 与 vacuous 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The apartment remained vacant for months.
confusable_words.difference_note: vacant 多指位置空着或表情茫然；vacuous 批评内容空洞。

extension.root_affix: vacu- 有 empty 的联想，帮助理解“空洞”。

word_family.example_sentence: A vacuum contains almost no matter.
The company announced a vacancy.

headline.core_en: worth remembering because it is special or unusual

familiar_bridge.summary: memory 是“记忆”；memorable 是容易留在记忆里的。

core.main: memorable 常形容 day、experience、speech、performance。

usage_groups.group_title: 经历、日子或表演令人难忘
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某事因为特别、强烈或不寻常而留在记忆里。

examples.sentence: The experience was memorable for all of us.
examples.translation: 这一经历让我们大家都难以忘怀。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书。

examples.sentence: Her speech ended with a memorable image of the city.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The event was notable for its scale.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: notable 强调值得注意，不一定有情感记忆。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study produced remarkable results.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: remarkable 更强调不寻常。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speech was polite but forgettable.
antonyms.difference_note: forgettable 与 memorable 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The city built a memorial for the victims.

headline.core_cn: 闪耀的；精彩的；带气泡的
headline.core_en: shining brightly, lively and excellent, or containing bubbles

familiar_bridge.summary: spark 是“火花”；sparkling 可以说光闪耀，也可以说表演精彩。

source.raw_ocr_text: 项词符炎 vt， 把 (责任或任务等 ) 委派给 (下级 ); 授予 (权力 entrust (a task or responsibility) to another person, typically one who is less senior than oneself: Congress delegated all the emergency powers to this president. 国会授予总统一切应变权力 。 | He delegates a lot of work. 他把很多工作委托别人去做

headline.core_en: to give a task or power to someone else; a person chosen to represent others

familiar_bridge.summary: give 是“给”；delegate 是把任务、责任或权力交给别人处理，常带上下级或组织分工。

core.main: delegate 作动词时重点是“把工作/权力交出去”；作名词时是“代表”。

usage_groups.group_title: 把任务或权力委派给别人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 上级、组织或机构把工作、责任、权力交给别人处理。

examples.sentence: Congress delegated emergency powers to the president.
examples.translation: 国会把应急权力授予总统。

examples.sentence: A good manager knows when to delegate routine tasks.
examples.translation: 好的管理者知道什么时候把常规任务委派出去。
examples.teaching_note: delegate work/tasks 是职场常见用法。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作为名词，指被选出代表某个团体、地区或会议的人。

examples.sentence: Each state sent a delegate to the convention.
examples.translation: 每个州都派了一名代表参加大会。
examples.teaching_note: 名词义是代表。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher assigned each student a role.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: assign 更普通；delegate 常涉及责任或权力下放。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She entrusted the files to her assistant.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: entrust 强调信任对方处理。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform devolved power to local councils.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: devolve 更正式，常指权力下放。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law empowers inspectors to close unsafe sites.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: empower 强调给予权力或能力。

antonyms.example_sentence: The director retained final authority.
antonyms.difference_note: retain 与把权力交出去相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The delegate spoke for the workers.
confusable_words.difference_note: 名词 delegate 是代表；动词 delegate 是委派。

word_family.example_sentence: The delegation met with the mayor.
Delegated authority must be used responsibly.
The delegate voted against the proposal.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The constitution devolves some powers to local governments.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 好斗的; 好争吵的 eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight: the pugnacious nature of the candidates 候选人好斗的本质 i 5044] truculent iRE pugnacity /paginasoti/ n. 好斗; 好争吵 the quality of being pugnacious iff X34] belligerence

headline.core_en: eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight

familiar_bridge.summary: fight 是“打斗”；pugnacious 形容人或性格一有机会就想争、想斗。

core.main: pugnacious 常形容 person、candidate、temper、style，带负面或强烈批评色彩。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 容易争论、挑衅或把互动变成冲突。

examples.sentence: The pugnacious candidate attacked every rival on the stage.
examples.translation: 这位好斗的候选人在台上攻击每个对手。
examples.teaching_note: candidate 与 pugnacious 很自然。

examples.sentence: His pugnacious tone made a calm discussion impossible.
examples.translation: 他好斗的语气让平静讨论变得不可能。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容语气或风格。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The argumentative student challenged every point.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: argumentative 偏爱争论；pugnacious 更有攻击性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The quarrelsome neighbor complained about everything.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: quarrelsome 更日常，强调爱吵。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The belligerent speech alarmed diplomats.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: belligerent 可用于国家或人，语气更强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The guard answered in a truculent voice.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: truculent 更书面，带粗暴威胁感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The peaceable crowd left quietly.
antonyms.difference_note: peaceable 与 pugnacious 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The lawyer adopted a combative style.
confusable_words.difference_note: combative 可用于辩论风格，未必像 pugnacious 那样性格化。

word_family.example_sentence: The candidate's pugnacity worried party leaders.
He responded pugnaciously to the criticism.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The speech was criticized for its belligerence.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. +t; 忧郁 a feeling of pensive sadness, typically with no obvious cause: an air of melancholy 忧者的气质派生 melancholic /melan kplik/ adj. 忧伤的; 忧郁的 feeling or expressing pensive sadness: His work often has a melancholic mood. 他的作品常有一种忧伤的情绪

headline.core_en: a thoughtful sadness, often without a clear cause

familiar_bridge.summary: sadness 是“悲伤”；melancholy 更安静、更沉思，不一定有明确原因。

core.main: melancholy 可作名词表示忧郁感，也可作形容词表示气氛、音乐、作品忧伤。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 一种低沉、若有所思的悲伤感，常没有明确原因。

examples.sentence: The old house had an air of melancholy.
examples.translation: 这座老房子有一种忧郁的气息。

examples.sentence: His work often has a melancholic mood.
examples.translation: 他的作品常有一种忧伤的情绪。
examples.teaching_note: melancholic 是派生形容词。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her sadness was obvious after the news.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sadness 更普通；melancholy 更安静、沉思。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A sense of gloom filled the room.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: gloom 更暗沉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Repeated failure led to despondency.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: despondency 更强调失去希望。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: There was wistfulness in her smile.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wistfulness 常带怀旧或渴望。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her cheerfulness lifted the mood.
antonyms.difference_note: cheerfulness 与 melancholy 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The study examines depression among adolescents.
confusable_words.difference_note: depression 可指临床抑郁；melancholy 更文学或情绪化。

word_family.example_sentence: The poem has a melancholic tone.
The novel portrays a period of melancholia.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: Her wistful expression suggested old regret.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 废弃的; 被淘汰的 no longer produced or used; out of date: Wooden warships are obsolete. 7 fl OLE EB 3044 outmoded

headline.core_en: no longer used or produced because something newer has replaced it

familiar_bridge.summary: old 是“旧”；obsolete 是旧到已经不再使用或被淘汰。

core.main: obsolete 常形容 technology、equipment、law、method，强调被新时代或新工具替代。

usage_groups.group_title: 技术、设备或方法被淘汰
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某物不再生产或使用，因为已经过时。

examples.sentence: Wooden warships are obsolete.
examples.translation: 木制军舰已经被淘汰。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书。

examples.sentence: The software became obsolete after the new system was introduced.
examples.translation: 新系统推出后，这款软件就过时了。
examples.teaching_note: become obsolete 很常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The information in the guide is outdated.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: outdated 强调信息或工具不新；obsolete 更强调已经不用。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The old-fashioned rule frustrated new employees.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: old-fashioned 可能只是旧派；obsolete 更接近淘汰。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The factory relied on outmoded machinery.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: outmoded 和 obsolete 接近，偏不合现代需要。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report uses current data.
antonyms.difference_note: current 与 obsolete 的过时相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The hospital bought modern equipment.
antonyms.difference_note: modern 与 obsolete 对照明显。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The antiquated custom no longer fits modern life.
confusable_words.difference_note: antiquated 可指陈旧不合时宜；obsolete 更强调不用或淘汰。

word_family.example_sentence: The obsolescence of the device was inevitable.
The obsolescent technology still survived in a few factories.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The policy seemed outmoded by the 1990s.

source.raw_ocr_text: IAL vt. 探寻 … 的起源; 描述 … 的发展 find or describe the origin or development of: Her book traces the development of art through the ages. 她的蔬讲述了艺术在历史上的发展历程 。 n. fect; [ 作定语 ] 微量的 a very small quantity, especially one too small to be accurately measured: trace element 微量元素

headline.core_en: to find or describe the origin or development of something; a very small amount

familiar_bridge.summary: track 是“追踪”；trace 更常说追溯来源、发展轨迹，也可作名词表示“微量”。

core.main: trace 有两个重点：动词追溯 origin/development；名词作定语表示微量，如 trace element。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 找出某事从哪里来、如何发展。

examples.sentence: Her book traces the development of art through the ages.
examples.translation: 她的书讲述了艺术在历史中的发展。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书。

examples.sentence: The historian traced the idea back to an earlier movement.
examples.translation: 历史学家把这个思想追溯到更早的运动。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示极少量物质的存在，重点是量很小，但仍然足以被检测、记录或影响判断。

examples.sentence: The water contained trace amounts of lead.
examples.translation: 水中含有微量铅。
examples.teaching_note: trace amounts 是科学语境。

usage_groups.student_note: 这里的 trace 是名词或形容词用法，不是“追踪”，而是“微量”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The researchers tracked changes in temperature.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: track 强调持续跟踪；trace 强调追溯来源或路线。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The test detected traces of the chemical.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: detect 强调发现存在。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team reconstructed the history of the site.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reconstruct 更强调把过去过程重建出来。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team lost track of the original source.
antonyms.difference_note: 和 trace 的追踪/追溯方向相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Patience is a useful trait.
confusable_words.difference_note: trait 形近但意思是特征。

word_family.example_sentence: The error was traceable to a faulty assumption.
The tracing of the family line took years.
The tracer revealed the path of the fluid.

related_words.note: 语义相近。
related_words.example_sentence: The satellite tracked the storm.

source.raw_ocr_text: PAGAL adj. 常规的; 例行公事的 performed as part of a regular procedure rather than for a special reason: a routine luggage search 常规行李检查 adj. 司空见惯的; 普通的; 常见的 of a commonplace or repetitious character; ordinary: routine problems 常见的问题

headline.core_en: done as part of a regular procedure; ordinary and repeated

familiar_bridge.summary: regular 是“规律的”；routine 是按惯例做的，可能中性，也可能带“平常、没新意”。

core.main: routine 可形容检查、任务、问题，也可作名词表示日常惯例。

usage_groups.group_title: 常规程序中的例行事项
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 按固定程序进行，不是特别原因。

examples.sentence: The guard made a routine luggage search.
examples.translation: 警卫进行了常规行李检查。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 事情太常见，缺少新意或特殊性。

examples.sentence: The office handles routine problems without a manager.
examples.translation: 办公室不用经理也能处理常见问题。
examples.teaching_note: routine problems 对应原书。

examples.sentence: Exercise became part of her daily routine.
examples.translation: 锻炼成了她日常惯例的一部分。
examples.teaching_note: 名词义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The office holds regular meetings.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: regular 强调有规律；routine 强调按惯例或重复。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was an ordinary day at school.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: ordinary 对应 routine 的平常义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His habitual caution slowed the process.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: habitual 强调习惯形成。

antonyms.example_sentence: The visit required special permission.
antonyms.difference_note: special 与 routine 的例行相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The results were exceptional.
antonyms.difference_note: exceptional 强调非平常。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The class relied too much on rote learning.
confusable_words.difference_note: rote 是死记硬背，和 routine 不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The equipment is routinely inspected.
The company routinized the approval process.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 routine 的程序感。
related_words.example_sentence: Regular maintenance prevents failures.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 威信; 威望; 好名声 widespread respect and admiration felt for someone or something on the basis of a perception of their achievements or quality: build up one's prestige 建立威 % | decline in prestige 威信下降 HE prestigious /pre'stidzos/ adj. 有威信的; ARH; 有声望的 inspiring respect and admiration; having high status: the most prestigious school in this country 这个国家中最享盛名的学校 | cheaper quarters in less prestigious parts of a town #24 FB RF He Ky BE 住房

headline.core_en: respect and admiration based on achievement, quality, or status

familiar_bridge.summary: respect 是“尊重”；prestige 是一个人、学校、职业或机构积累出的社会声望。

core.main: prestige 常和 build、gain、lose、decline in 搭配，也可修饰 profession、university、brand。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 因成就、质量或地位而获得广泛尊重。

examples.sentence: The university gained prestige after several major discoveries.
examples.translation: 几项重大发现之后，这所大学声望上升。
examples.teaching_note: gain prestige 很自然。

examples.sentence: A series of scandals caused a decline in the mayor's prestige.
examples.translation: 一连串丑闻导致市长威望下降。

usage_groups.group_title: 有声望的机构、地区或职位
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容某物在社会上地位高、受尊敬。

examples.sentence: They moved to a less prestigious part of town to save money.
examples.translation: 为了省钱，他们搬到了城里声望较低的区域。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书派生例句方向。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The school has a strong reputation for science.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reputation 可好可坏；prestige 通常是正面声望。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The award raised her status in the field.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: status 是地位；prestige 是由地位和成就带来的尊敬。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The artist gained international renown.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renown 更书面，强调广为人知的名声。

antonyms.example_sentence: The scandal brought the office into disrepute.
antonyms.difference_note: disrepute 与 prestige 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She won a prestigious fellowship.
confusable_words.difference_note: prestigious 是形容词；prestige 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The award is highly prestigious.
The program is prestigiously funded by several foundations.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 prestige。
related_words.example_sentence: The company protected its reputation carefully.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 模糊的; 不清楚的; 模棱两可的 (of a concept or idea) unclear, vague, or ill-defined: nebulous memory 模糊的记忆 23044 vague

headline.core_en: vague, unclear, or not well defined

familiar_bridge.summary: vague 是“模糊的”；nebulous 更像雾一样没有清楚边界，常形容 idea、plan、memory。

core.main: nebulous 批评概念、计划或记忆不清楚、不成形。

usage_groups.group_title: 概念、计划或记忆模糊不清
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 边界不清、内容不明确，让人难以把握。

examples.sentence: The proposal was based on a nebulous idea of progress.
examples.translation: 这项提案建立在一个模糊的“进步”概念上。
examples.teaching_note: a nebulous idea 很自然。

examples.sentence: Only a nebulous memory of the meeting remained.
examples.translation: 关于那次会议只剩下模糊记忆。
examples.teaching_note: nebulous memory 对应原书。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His answer was vague and unhelpful.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: vague 最普通；nebulous 更书面、更有雾状边界不清感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were unclear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unclear 更直接，语气普通。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The phrase was ambiguous.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ambiguous 强调可有多种解释。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The project had ill-defined goals.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ill-defined 强调定义或边界不清。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speaker gave a clear explanation.
antonyms.difference_note: clear 与 nebulous 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The scientist gave a precise measurement.
antonyms.difference_note: precise 强调准确无模糊。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The telescope captured a distant nebula.
confusable_words.difference_note: nebula 是天文名词；nebulous 是形容词。

extension.root_affix: nebul- 与 cloud/fog 的画面有关，帮助理解“模糊如云雾”。

word_family.example_sentence: The nebula glowed faintly in the image.
The photograph showed a nebular shape.

related_words.note: 基础近义词。
related_words.example_sentence: The instructions were vague.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 对 … 表示不满; 抱怨 express discontent or sorrow over (something): He bemoans the fact that the team lost again. 他抱怨球队又输了

headline.core_en: to complain about or express sorrow over something

familiar_bridge.summary: moan 是“呻吟/抱怨”；bemoan 更正式，表示为某事遗憾、哀叹或抱怨。

core.main: bemoan 常接 loss、decline、fact、failure，语气比 complain 更书面。

usage_groups.group_title: 对损失、失败或坏结果表示遗憾
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 带着不满或伤感抱怨某事。

examples.sentence: He bemoans the fact that the team lost again.
examples.translation: 他抱怨球队又输了。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书。

examples.sentence: Critics bemoaned the decline of public debate.
examples.translation: 批评者哀叹公共辩论的衰落。
examples.teaching_note: bemoan the decline of 很自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Residents complained about the noise.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: complain 更日常；bemoan 更正式、更带遗憾。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The writer lamented the loss of local traditions.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lament 和 bemoan 接近，但更偏悲伤痛惜。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee deplored the violence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deplore 更正式，常带道德谴责。

antonyms.example_sentence: The festival celebrates local culture.
antonyms.difference_note: celebrate 与 bemoan 的负面情绪相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He moaned about the cold weather.
confusable_words.difference_note: moan 更口语，可指呻吟；bemoan 更书面。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: The patient moaned in pain.
The article's bemoaning of decline felt excessive.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The poem laments the loss of youth.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 完全反驳; 彻底否定 comprehensively refute (an argument or its proponent): The research demolished several myths about the disease. 研究彻底反驳了关于这一疾病的谣言

headline.core_en: to destroy a building, or to completely refute an argument

familiar_bridge.summary: destroy 是“摧毁”；demolish 可以拆建筑，也可以把论点彻底驳倒。

core.main: 原书重点是论证义：evidence/research demolishes myths or arguments。字面义“拆毁建筑”也很常见。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用证据把错误说法完全推翻。

examples.sentence: The research demolished several myths about the disease.
examples.translation: 研究彻底驳倒了关于这种疾病的几个谣言。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书。

examples.sentence: One document demolished the witness's entire argument.
examples.translation: 一份文件彻底推翻了证人的整个论点。
examples.teaching_note: demolish an argument 是 GRE 语境常见。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把建筑物或结构物拆掉、毁掉。

examples.sentence: The city demolished the unsafe bridge.
examples.translation: 市政府拆除了那座不安全的桥。
examples.teaching_note: 字面义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fire destroyed the warehouse.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: destroy 范围很宽；demolish 常指拆建筑或彻底驳倒。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence refuted the accusation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: refute 是论证层面的反驳。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The invading army razed the village.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: raze 专指彻底毁掉建筑或城市。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment disproved the theory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disprove 强调证明错误。

antonyms.example_sentence: The town built a new school.
antonyms.difference_note: build 与拆毁义相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data support the hypothesis.
antonyms.difference_note: support 与驳倒论点相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The demolition of the old stadium took weeks.
confusable_words.difference_note: demolition 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The demolition of the factory changed the neighborhood.
The demolished claim no longer convinced readers.

related_words.note: 论证义相关。
related_words.example_sentence: The evidence refuted the rumor.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 意见分歧; 不和 disagreement that leads to discord: cause dissension within terrorist ranks 在恐怖分子队伍中引起纷争 | sow dissension among friends EMA KZ APRS lA

headline.core_en: serious disagreement within a group

familiar_bridge.summary: disagree 是不同意；dissension 更强调一个群体内部因为意见不合而产生裂痕。

core.main: dissension 通常不是普通小争论，而是团队、党派、组织内部的分歧和不和。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调共同体内部意见不一致，可能削弱合作或统一。

examples.sentence: Internal dissension weakened the reform movement.
examples.translation: 内部意见分歧削弱了这场改革运动。
examples.teaching_note: internal dissension 常用于组织内部不和。

examples.sentence: The leader tried to suppress dissension within the party.
examples.translation: 领导者试图压制党内分歧。
examples.teaching_note: within the party 点出分歧发生在同一阵营内部。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal caused disagreement among the committee members.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disagreement 最普通；dissension 更强调群体内部不和。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy created discord within the coalition.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: discord 语气更强，可指关系紧张。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The dispute produced a schism in the organization.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: schism 指更严重、更制度化的分裂。

antonyms.example_sentence: After hours of debate, the group finally reached consensus.
antonyms.difference_note: consensus 是意见达成一致，与 dissension 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The chair tried to restore harmony after the argument.
antonyms.difference_note: harmony 强调关系和顺，没有明显不和。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Several judges wrote opinions of dissent.
confusable_words.difference_note: dissent 更常指个人或少数派明确反对；dissension 强调整体内部不和。

parts.note: 帮助理解“意见分开”的感觉。

word_family.note: 同源相关词，常指明确反对意见。
word_family.example_sentence: Public dissent grew after the announcement.
The dissenter refused to sign the statement.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 《对于权威或公认意见 ) 持异议; 不同意 the expression or holding of opinions at variance with those previously, commonly, or officially held: There was no dissent from this view. 没有人对此表示异议

headline.core_en: disagreement with an official or majority view

familiar_bridge.summary: agree 是同意；dissent 是站出来说“我不同意”，尤其是反对多数意见或官方立场。

core.main: dissent 可以是名词“异议”，也可以作动词“持异议”；重点是公开或明确地不同意。

usage_groups.group_title: 对多数意见或权威表示异议
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于个人、群体或法官不同意主流判断、政策或决定。

examples.sentence: Several scientists dissented from the committee’s conclusion.
examples.translation: 几位科学家不同意委员会的结论。
examples.teaching_note: dissent from a conclusion/view 是正式表达。

examples.sentence: The judge wrote a powerful dissenting opinion.
examples.translation: 这位法官写了一篇有力的异议意见。
examples.teaching_note: dissenting opinion 是法律语境常见搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She disagreed with the final decision.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disagree 更日常；dissent 更正式，常带公开反对意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Several residents objected to the new road.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: object 常针对具体计划提出反对。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The senator demurred when asked to endorse the bill.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: demur 更书面，常指谨慎或正式反对。

antonyms.example_sentence: The board gave its assent to the proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: assent 是正式同意，与 dissent 相对。

antonyms.example_sentence: Participants must consent before the study begins.
antonyms.difference_note: consent 强调许可或同意参与。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Dissension within the party delayed the vote.
confusable_words.difference_note: dissension 是内部关系不和；dissent 是具体异议或反对立场。

word_family.example_sentence: The dissenters argued that the rule was unfair.
Her dissenting view later gained support.

word_family.note: 同源相关词，偏群体内部不和。
word_family.example_sentence: The dispute caused dissension among the members.

source.raw_ocr_text: AAAEX adj. 私密的; 个人的 private and personal: intimate correspondence 私密信件 vt， 暗示; 透露 imply or hint: He intimated to me his intention of resigning. 他隐约向我透露了辞职的意图 。 | Her smile intimated that she was pleased. 她的微笑表示她是满意的

headline.core_en: close and private; to suggest something indirectly

familiar_bridge.summary: private 是私人的；intimate 作形容词时更亲近、更私密。作动词时，它不是直接说，而是“暗示”。

core.main: intimate 有两个常见方向：形容词表示亲密、私密；动词表示不直接说破，而是含蓄透露。

usage_groups.group_title: 亲密或私密的关系、场景
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容关系、谈话、细节或空间很私人、很靠近。

examples.sentence: The memoir reveals intimate details of her childhood.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录透露了她童年的私密细节。
examples.teaching_note: intimate details 指不适合公开、很个人的信息。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作动词时表示不直接说出，而是通过表情、语气或措辞让人明白。

examples.sentence: Her silence intimated that she did not approve.
examples.translation: 她的沉默暗示她并不赞成。
examples.teaching_note: intimate that 后接暗示出的内容。

examples.sentence: He intimated his intention to resign.
examples.translation: 他含蓄地透露了辞职的打算。
examples.teaching_note: intimate an intention 是正式用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They have been close friends for years.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: close 更普通；intimate 更私密、更深入。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The letter contained private information.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: private 强调不公开；intimate 常带情感或关系上的亲近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report included confidential details.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: confidential 强调被限制传播，不一定亲密。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article insinuated that the official had lied.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: insinuate 常带负面、拐弯抹角的暗示；intimate 更中性正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: Their relationship became distant after the dispute.
antonyms.difference_note: distant 与亲密相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were explicit and easy to follow.
antonyms.difference_note: explicit 与动词 intimate 的“暗示”相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Children often imitate adults.
confusable_words.difference_note: imitate 拼写相近但完全不同义；intimate 是亲密/暗示。

word_family.example_sentence: The letters reveal a surprising intimacy.
The two issues are intimately connected.

word_family.note: 与动词 intimate 的“暗示”相关。
word_family.example_sentence: There was no intimation that he planned to leave.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i.&v.t. 想象; 设想 form a mental representation of; imagine: We could not conceive of such things happening to us. 我们无法想象这种事发生在我们身上 。 派生 conceivable /kan'si:vobl/ adj. 能想象出来的; 有可能的 capable of being imagined or grasped mentally: every conceivable means 一切能想到的办法 | the worst conceivable result 可以想象得到的最坏结果 i 044 plausible

headline.core_en: to imagine, form, or understand an idea; to become pregnant

familiar_bridge.summary: imagine 是想象；conceive 更像在脑中形成一个想法、计划或可能性。

core.main: conceive 的重点是“在头脑中形成”。正式语境常说 conceive of a possibility 或 conceive a plan。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于想象、理解或形成一个抽象想法。

examples.sentence: It is difficult to conceive of a society without rules.
examples.translation: 很难设想一个没有规则的社会。
examples.teaching_note: conceive of 表示在脑中设想某种可能。

examples.sentence: The theory conceives of language as a social tool.
examples.translation: 这个理论把语言理解为一种社会工具。
examples.teaching_note: conceive of A as B 表示“把 A 理解为 B”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一个想法、项目或方案最初在头脑中形成。

examples.sentence: The architect conceived a building that could adapt to the climate.
examples.translation: 建筑师构想了一座能够适应气候的建筑。
examples.teaching_note: conceive a building/project 指构想设计。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 生物语境中指受孕，和“形成生命”有关。

examples.sentence: The couple had been trying to conceive for several years.
examples.translation: 这对夫妇多年来一直尝试怀孕。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项常见但不是多数 GRE 语境重点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: I cannot imagine life without music.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: imagine 更日常；conceive 更正式，偏形成概念或可能性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She thought of a simpler solution.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: think of 更口语，conceive 更抽象正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The planners envisioned a city with fewer cars.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: envision 强调在脑中看到未来图景。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team formulated a new strategy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: formulate 更强调把想法组织成方案或表达。

antonyms.example_sentence: Critics misconceived the purpose of the reform.
antonyms.difference_note: misconceive 是形成错误理解。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Readers may perceive a shift in tone.
confusable_words.difference_note: perceive 是感知到；conceive 是在头脑中形成。

word_family.example_sentence: His conception of justice changed over time.
It is conceivable that the policy will fail.
The public misconceived the scientist’s claim.
The study challenged preconceived assumptions.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 看法; (对某事的 ) 理解 the sum of a person's ideas and beliefs concerning something: a child's conception of responsibility 孩子对于责任的理解 i misconception /miskon'sepfan/ n. 误解; 错误的看法 a view or opinion that is incorrect because based on faulty thinking or understanding: a popular misconception about science 对于科学的误解 | Public misconceptions about AIDS remain high. 公众对于艾滋病的误解依

headline.core_en: an idea or understanding of something; the beginning or formation of something

familiar_bridge.summary: concept 是概念；conception 更强调一个人或时代对某事形成的理解方式。

core.main: GRE 阅读里 conception 常指“对某个问题的理解/观念”，不是简单 idea 列表。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调不是零散想法，而是一套理解事物的方式或观念框架。

examples.sentence: The essay challenges the traditional conception of progress.
examples.translation: 这篇文章挑战了传统的进步观。
examples.teaching_note: conception of progress 是阅读中常见抽象搭配。

examples.sentence: Her conception of leadership emphasizes cooperation rather than control.
examples.translation: 她对领导力的理解强调合作而不是控制。
examples.teaching_note: conception 指理解框架。

usage_groups.group_title: 想法、计划或生命的形成
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 可指项目、理论或生命的最初形成。

examples.sentence: From its conception, the project was designed to serve rural communities.
examples.translation: 这个项目从构想之初就是为了服务农村社区。
examples.teaching_note: from its conception 指从最初构想开始。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The idea seemed simple at first.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: idea 更普通；conception 更强调成体系的理解。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her understanding of the issue deepened over time.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: understanding 偏理解过程或结果；conception 偏整体观念。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The book rejects the notion that talent alone explains success.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: notion 可指较笼统的观念；conception 往往更成体系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory offers a framework for analyzing culture.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: framework 强调分析结构，不一定是个人观念。

antonyms.example_sentence: The lecture corrected a common misconception about evolution.
antonyms.difference_note: misconception 是错误的 conception。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The concept of freedom varies across cultures.
confusable_words.difference_note: concept 是单个概念；conception 更像对概念的一套理解。

word_family.example_sentence: The concept is difficult to define.
The artist conceived the piece as a protest.
Every conceivable option was discussed.
The article addresses several misconceptions.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 无休止的; 毫不放松的; 坚定不移的 not yielding in strength, severity, or determination: unrelenting opposition 坚决的反对 | The heat was unrelenting. 炎热丝毫没有减弱 。 近义词 persistent

headline.core_cn: 不停的；不松懈的；不屈的
headline.core_en: not stopping, weakening, or becoming less severe

familiar_bridge.summary: relent 是缓和、让步；unrelenting 就是不缓和、不松手。

core.main: unrelenting 可以形容压力、批评、努力、天气等持续不断，也可以形容人或态度不让步。

usage_groups.group_title: 压力、攻击或困难持续不减
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某种外部压力一直存在，没有减弱。

examples.sentence: The workers faced unrelenting pressure to meet the deadline.
examples.translation: 工人们面临着持续不断的期限压力。
examples.teaching_note: unrelenting pressure 强调压力不减。

examples.sentence: The unrelenting heat made the journey exhausting.
examples.translation: 持续不减的高温让旅程令人疲惫。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容天气或环境条件。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容人、追求或努力坚定持续，不轻易退让。

examples.sentence: Her unrelenting pursuit of evidence eventually changed the debate.
examples.translation: 她对证据的不懈追寻最终改变了这场辩论。
examples.teaching_note: unrelenting pursuit 可带褒义，强调坚持。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Persistent rain delayed the harvest.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: persistent 更普通；unrelenting 语气更强，强调完全不减弱。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The constant noise made concentration difficult.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: constant 强调一直存在；unrelenting 还带压力或强度。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The inexorable spread of the disease alarmed officials.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inexorable 强调无法阻止的趋势。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform faced implacable opposition.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: implacable 常形容敌意或反对无法缓和。

antonyms.example_sentence: After hours of debate, the opposition showed signs of relenting.
antonyms.difference_note: relenting 与 unrelenting 正相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Intermittent rain fell throughout the day.
antonyms.difference_note: intermittent 强调断断续续，不是持续不断。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The campaign faced relentless criticism.
confusable_words.difference_note: relentless 与 unrelenting 很近；relentless 常更强调强度和压迫感。

word_family.example_sentence: The storm finally relented after midnight.
The relentless pace exhausted the team.
The reporter pursued the story unrelentingly.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 不停的; 无休止的 oppressively constant; incessant: the relentless heat of the desert 沙漠的

headline.core_cn: 不停的；无情的；持续强烈的
headline.core_en: continuing with strong force and not letting up

familiar_bridge.summary: relent 是缓和；relentless 就是完全不缓和，常给人持续压迫感。

core.main: relentless 常形容攻击、批评、追求、压力、速度等持续不断而且强度很高。

usage_groups.group_title: 攻击、批评或压力持续不断
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一波接一波、没有停顿，常让人感到被压迫。

examples.sentence: The mayor faced relentless criticism after the scandal.
examples.translation: 丑闻之后，市长遭到持续不断的批评。
examples.teaching_note: relentless criticism 强调批评持续且强烈。

examples.sentence: Relentless pressure from investors forced the company to change its plan.
examples.translation: 投资者持续不断的压力迫使公司改变计划。
examples.teaching_note: pressure 与 relentless 搭配自然。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 也可形容努力、追赶或进程持续推进。

examples.sentence: The scientist’s relentless pursuit of a solution eventually paid off.
examples.translation: 这位科学家坚持不懈地寻找解决方案，最终取得了成果。
examples.teaching_note: relentless 可带褒义，表示不放弃。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team’s momentum seemed unstoppable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unstoppable 强调无法阻止；relentless 强调持续强烈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Persistent doubts remained after the report.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: persistent 语气较弱，不一定有压迫感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The incessant noise kept everyone awake.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incessant 常形容声音、抱怨等不停。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The patient required unremitting care.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unremitting 更正式，强调没有间断。

antonyms.example_sentence: Sporadic protests continued for several weeks.
antonyms.difference_note: sporadic 与 relentless 的持续性相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge was merciful in sentencing the defendant.
antonyms.difference_note: merciful 与 relentless 的无情感相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The region suffered under unrelenting heat.
confusable_words.difference_note: 两者很近；relentless 常更有强烈推进或攻击感。

word_family.example_sentence: The opposition refused to relent.
The campaign attacked the candidate relentlessly.
The relentlessness of the questioning surprised him.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 令人愉悦的 enjoyable and pleasurable; pleasant: an agreeable atmosphere 友好的氛围 2344] amicable

headline.core_cn: 令人愉快的；愿意同意的
headline.core_en: pleasant; willing to accept or agree to something

familiar_bridge.summary: agree 是同意；agreeable 一方面可以说人或事“令人愉快”，另一方面可以说某人“愿意接受”。

core.main: agreeable 有两个常见方向：pleasant 的感觉，以及 willing 的态度。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容天气、声音、环境、人等让人觉得舒服或愉快。

examples.sentence: The town has an agreeable climate and quiet streets.
examples.translation: 这个小镇气候宜人，街道安静。
examples.teaching_note: agreeable climate 是“宜人的气候”。

examples.sentence: Her agreeable manner made the meeting less tense.
examples.translation: 她友善得体的举止让会议没那么紧张。
examples.teaching_note: agreeable manner 形容让人舒服的态度。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容人愿意接受建议、安排或条件。

examples.sentence: The committee was agreeable to a short delay.
examples.translation: 委员会愿意接受短暂延期。
examples.teaching_note: be agreeable to 表示愿意接受。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We spent a pleasant afternoon by the river.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: pleasant 更普通；agreeable 更书面，常形容气候、举止或安排。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was a nice place to rest.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: nice 很日常；agreeable 更正式克制。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The host was amiable and generous.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: amiable 主要形容人性格友善。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She found the academic environment congenial.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: congenial 强调适合某人性格或兴趣。

antonyms.example_sentence: The room had a disagreeable smell.
antonyms.difference_note: disagreeable 是直接反义词。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness faced hostile questioning.
antonyms.difference_note: hostile 强调敌意，比 disagreeable 更强。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The two sides reached an amicable settlement.
confusable_words.difference_note: amicable 常形容关系或解决方式和睦；agreeable 可形容事物令人愉快或人愿意接受。

word_family.example_sentence: The experts agree on the main point.
The parties signed an agreement.
The task was disagreeable but necessary.
The evening passed agreeably.

source.raw_ocr_text: phr. 反之亦然 with the main items in the preceding statement the other way around: Women may bring their husbands with them, and vice versa. XH] VAt# LAMA KA, RZ AT WET HL.

headline.core_en: with the order or relation reversed

familiar_bridge.summary: vice versa 就是“反过来也一样”：A 影响 B，B 也可能影响 A。

core.main: 它用于避免重复说一遍相反方向的关系，常放在句末。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 前一句说 A 对 B 的关系，vice versa 表示 B 对 A 也有同样关系。

examples.sentence: Teachers influence students, and vice versa.
examples.translation: 老师影响学生，学生也会反过来影响老师。
examples.teaching_note: and vice versa 避免重复说“students influence teachers”。

examples.sentence: Economic policy can shape public opinion, and vice versa.
examples.translation: 经济政策会影响公众意见，反过来公众意见也会影响经济政策。
examples.teaching_note: 适合表达双向影响。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: High prices may reduce demand; conversely, low prices may increase it.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: conversely 常用于逻辑转折；vice versa 强调双方位置互换后也成立。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Parents shape children’s habits, and the reverse is also true.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 这是 vice versa 的解释性表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The two institutions supported each other reciprocally.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reciprocally 强调相互作用或互惠关系，不一定用于句末省略。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Anna and Ben scored 90 and 85, respectively.
confusable_words.difference_note: respectively 是按顺序对应，不是“反之亦然”。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 vice versa 的“换方向”。
related_words.example_sentence: The reverse argument is also possible.

related_words.note: 逻辑和数学语境中常见。
related_words.example_sentence: The converse of the statement is not always true.

source.raw_ocr_text: vit. (将权力 、 工作等 ) 移交给下级; 下放 transfer or delegate (power) to a lower level, especially from central government to local or regional administration: devolve power to the provinces 将权力下放到省里 v.i. (devolve into) 逐渐恶化; 逐渐退化 degenerate or be split into: Order devolves into chaos. 秩序变成了混乱

headline.core_en: to transfer power or responsibility; to degenerate into a worse state

familiar_bridge.summary: delegate 是委派任务；devolve 更正式，常指权力下放，也可指局面退化成混乱。

core.main: devolve 有两个重要方向：权力/责任转移，以及状况变坏。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 政府、组织或上级把权力、责任移交给较低层级。

examples.sentence: The reform devolved more power to local councils.
examples.translation: 这项改革把更多权力下放给地方议会。
examples.teaching_note: devolve power to... 是政治/制度语境常见用法。

examples.sentence: Responsibility for the program devolved to regional offices.
examples.translation: 这个项目的责任移交给了地区办公室。
examples.teaching_note: devolve to 表示责任落到某一方。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 讨论、秩序或局面从较有组织的状态滑向混乱或低水平。

examples.sentence: The debate quickly devolved into a shouting match.
examples.translation: 辩论很快退化成了互相喊叫。
examples.teaching_note: devolve into 表示变成更糟、更低级的状态。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency transferred responsibility to a local office.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: transfer 更普通；devolve 常指权力或责任正式下放。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The manager delegated the task to an assistant.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: delegate 多指把任务交给某人；devolve 常指制度层级上的权力转移。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform decentralized control over schools.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: decentralize 强调从中心向地方分散权力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discussion degenerated into personal attacks.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: degenerate 与 devolve into 的恶化义接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new law centralized authority in the capital.
antonyms.difference_note: centralize 与权力下放相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The situation improved after mediation.
antonyms.difference_note: improve 与退化为更糟状态相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The tradition evolved over centuries.
confusable_words.difference_note: evolve 是发展变化，不一定变坏；devolve 常是下放或退化。

parts.note: 帮助理解“向下移交”或“下降退化”的方向。
同根见 evolve/revolve，表示变化或转动。

word_family.example_sentence: The region demanded greater devolution of power.
Devolved authority allowed towns to set their own rules.

related_words.note: 同根 volve，但方向不一定变坏。
related_words.example_sentence: The language evolved over time.

related_words.note: 同根 volve，保留“转”的含义。
related_words.example_sentence: The debate revolved around funding.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 和急躁的; 443849; 冲动的 acting or done quickly and without thought or care: be impetuous in one's habits 生性急躁 | Children are more impetuous than adults. 孩子比成人莽撞

headline.core_en: acting quickly without enough thought

familiar_bridge.summary: impulse 是一时冲动；impetuous 形容人或决定被冲动推着走，来不及冷静判断。

core.main: impetuous 强调行动太快、太凭一时情绪，常带“缺少审慎”的负面评价。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容人、决定、行动没有充分思考就发生。

examples.sentence: His impetuous decision to quit left him without a plan.
examples.translation: 他冲动辞职，结果没有任何后续计划。
examples.teaching_note: impetuous decision 强调决定来得太快、欠考虑。

examples.sentence: The general’s impetuous attack exposed the army to unnecessary danger.
examples.translation: 将军的冲动进攻让军队暴露在不必要的危险中。
examples.teaching_note: GRE/历史语境中常评价行动缺少审慎。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was rash to invest all the money in one project.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rash 强调判断鲁莽；impetuous 更强调行动被冲动驱使。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She made an impulsive purchase and regretted it later.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: impulsive 更日常；impetuous 更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The country rushed headlong into war.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: headlong 强调不顾后果地向前冲。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The minister warned against precipitate action.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: precipitate 更正式，常形容行动过早、仓促。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee made a deliberate choice after months of study.
antonyms.difference_note: deliberate 与 impetuous 的冲动相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: A prudent investor avoids unnecessary risk.
antonyms.difference_note: prudent 强调考虑风险、审慎。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The discovery gave impetus to further research.
confusable_words.difference_note: impetus 是名词“推动力”；impetuous 是形容词“冲动的”。

word_family.note: 同源相关，保留“推动”的感觉。
word_family.example_sentence: The crisis gave new impetus to reform.
He acted impetuously and ignored the warning.
Her impetuosity often led to mistakes.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. Bit; 轻松的事 a thing of little importance; a very easy task: To him money was a bagatelle. 对他来说钱不重要

headline.core_en: something of little importance or value

familiar_bridge.summary: trifle 是小事；bagatelle 更书面，常带“根本不值得大惊小怪”的感觉。

core.main: bagatelle 指无足轻重的小事，或者在某人眼里不值一提的东西。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某事价值、重要性或难度很低，不值得认真担心。

examples.sentence: To a billionaire, the fine was a mere bagatelle.
examples.translation: 对亿万富翁来说，这笔罚款不过是小事一桩。
examples.teaching_note: a mere bagatelle 表示“不值一提的小事”。

examples.sentence: The repairs looked simple, but the cost was no bagatelle.
examples.translation: 维修看起来简单，但费用可不是小数目。
examples.teaching_note: no bagatelle 反过来表示“不是小事”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mistake was a trifle, not a serious failure.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trifle 更常见；bagatelle 更书面、略带轻描淡写。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay was a minor matter compared with the larger crisis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: minor matter 是解释性表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The debate focused on trivialities rather than policy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: triviality 强调不重要。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The workers were paid a pittance for difficult labor.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pittance 只指钱少；bagatelle 可指任何小事或小数目。

antonyms.example_sentence: The treaty was a matter of consequence for both nations.
antonyms.difference_note: 表示真正有影响的事，与 bagatelle 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Funding became a major issue in the campaign.
antonyms.difference_note: major issue 强调重要性高。

related_words.note: 帮助建立“微不足道”的语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: The report ignored trivial details.

related_words.note: 常见近义词。
related_words.example_sentence: He dismissed the complaint as a trifle.
