source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 产生; 带来; 引起 cause (something, especially an emotion or situation) to arise or come about: changes that are likely to generate controversy 容易导致争议的改变 | generate more jobs in the economy 制造更多工作岗位 3K generative /daensrstrv/ adj. 有生产能力的 having the power or function of generating, originating, producing, or reproducing: Fear is a generative passion. 丽惧可以产生其他情绪 。 派生 regenerate /ri'dzenoreit/ v.i.&v.t. 再生; 使再生 to generate or produce anew; especially to replace (a body part) by a new growth of tissue: Once destroyed, brain cells do not regenerate. 脑细胞一旦损坏就不会再生

headline.core_en: to produce or cause something to exist

familiar_bridge.summary: create 是创造；generate 更常说“产生某种结果、能量、想法或反应”。

core.main: generate 强调让某物出现，常用于结果、争议、收入、能量、数据等。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某事带来影响、情绪、讨论或问题。

examples.sentence: The new policy generated intense controversy.
examples.translation: 这项新政策引发了激烈争议。
examples.teaching_note: generate controversy 是正式常见搭配。

examples.sentence: The exhibit generated renewed interest in local history.
examples.translation: 这场展览重新激发了人们对地方史的兴趣。
examples.teaching_note: generate interest 表示引起兴趣。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 也可指系统、机器或活动产出具体资源或信息。

examples.sentence: The wind farm generates enough electricity for the town.
examples.translation: 这座风电场产生的电力足够供应全镇。
examples.teaching_note: generate electricity 是技术语境常见用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The factory produces medical equipment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: produce 更普通；generate 常强调结果或能量被引发出来。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The program created new jobs.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: create 更宽泛，generate 常用于影响、数据、能源等产出。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy engendered widespread resentment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: engender 更正式，常接情绪或社会反应。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The question elicited a surprising response.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: elicit 强调把反应引出来；generate 强调产生结果。

antonyms.example_sentence: The regime suppressed public debate.
antonyms.difference_note: suppress 阻止某物出现，与 generate 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The device consumes very little electricity.
antonyms.difference_note: consume 是消耗资源，不是产生资源。

word_family.example_sentence: The generation of electricity requires careful planning.

word_family.note: 产生某物的设备或程序。
word_family.example_sentence: The generator supplied power during the storm.
The theory has generative power.
Some tissues can regenerate after injury.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Sil; 仿制; 制作副本 to repeat or copy (something) exactly: /t might be impractical to replicate Eastern culture in the west. 在西方复制东方文化是不现实的 。 vit. [PEE] 重复 ( 研究或实验 ， 以期得出一致的结果 ) repeat (a scientific experiment or trial) to obtain a consistent result: Scientists have failed to replicate the results of his experiment. 其他科学家没能复制他的实验结果

headline.core_en: to copy something exactly or repeat a result

familiar_bridge.summary: copy 是复制；replicate 更正式，尤其常见于实验、研究和模型结果。

core.main: replicate 的核心是“按原样再做一次”，用来验证结果或制造副本。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调尽量按原样复制文化、设计、过程或模型。

examples.sentence: The museum tried to replicate the atmosphere of a nineteenth-century street.
examples.translation: 博物馆试图再现十九世纪街道的氛围。
examples.teaching_note: replicate atmosphere/model 是“再现”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 科研语境中，重复实验看结果是否能再次出现。

examples.sentence: Other researchers failed to replicate the results of the experiment.
examples.translation: 其他研究者没能重复出该实验的结果。
examples.teaching_note: replicate the results 是科学写作常见搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The student copied the diagram carefully.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: copy 更日常；replicate 更正式，强调精确再现。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lab repeated the test three times.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: repeat 强调再做一次；replicate 强调结果或结构与原来一致。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study reproduced the original findings.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reproduce 与 replicate 在科研语境接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The technician duplicated the file before editing it.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: duplicate 偏做出一份相同副本。

antonyms.example_sentence: Even a small change can alter the result.
antonyms.difference_note: alter 是改变，不是复制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company must innovate rather than merely copy.
antonyms.difference_note: innovate 强调创造新东西，与复制相对。

word_family.example_sentence: The model is a replica of the ancient ship.
Replication is essential for reliable science.
The result was not replicable.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 厌恶; 讨厌; MK dislike intensely: She not only disliked him, she detested him. 她不仅不喜欢他 ， 而且讨厌他 。 | Idetest cruelty. 我惜恶残忍 。 派生 detestation /di:tes'terfan/ n. 厌恶; WIR; HK intense dislike: He has a detestation of unpunctuality. 他讨厌不守时

headline.core_en: to dislike someone or something very strongly

familiar_bridge.summary: dislike 是不喜欢；detest 是强烈到“受不了、很厌恶”。

core.main: detest 比 dislike 强很多，常用于道德上、情感上强烈反感的人或事。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表达非常强烈的反感，语气接近 hate，但更正式。

examples.sentence: She detests cruelty in any form.
examples.translation: 她痛恨任何形式的残忍。
examples.teaching_note: detest cruelty 是道德反感。

examples.sentence: He detested being treated as if he were helpless.
examples.translation: 他很厌恶别人把他当成无助的人对待。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many people hate being interrupted.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hate 更日常；detest 更正式、更强烈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She dislikes loud restaurants.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: dislike 语气弱得多。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reformer abhorred corruption.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abhor 很正式，常指道德上强烈厌恶。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He loathed the idea of betraying a friend.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: loathe 与 detest 很近，语气强烈。

antonyms.example_sentence: The students admired her courage.
antonyms.difference_note: admire 是正向评价。

antonyms.example_sentence: She cherished the old letters.
antonyms.difference_note: cherish 表示珍惜，与厌恶相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The witness testified in court.
confusable_words.difference_note: 拼写中有 test，但 detest 与 testify 无语义关系。

word_family.example_sentence: His detestation of injustice shaped his career.
The novel portrays the villain as detestable.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: #4740 v.t. SCHL; 将 … 变为现实 cause (something desired or anticipated) to happen: She finally realized her goal. 她终于实现了她的目标 。 4X44 achieve

headline.core_en: to become aware of something; to make something real; to obtain money from an asset

familiar_bridge.summary: real 是真实的；realize 可以是“意识到事实”，也可以是“让目标成真”。

core.main: realize 是多义词：最常见是意识到；正式语境也常说 realize a goal，把目标变成现实。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 突然或逐渐明白某个事实、问题或意义。

examples.sentence: She realized that the data did not support the theory.
examples.translation: 她意识到数据并不支持这个理论。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 把目标或计划变成现实
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 正式表达中，指实现理想、目标或计划。

examples.sentence: The new funding helped the researchers realize their ambitious plan.
examples.translation: 新的资金帮助研究者实现了他们宏大的计划。
examples.teaching_note: realize a plan/goal 是“实现”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 金融/商业语境中指通过出售资产获得收益。

examples.sentence: The company sold the land to realize a profit.
examples.translation: 公司出售土地以实现盈利。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项较正式，注意不是“意识到”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He noticed a mistake in the report.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: notice 偏看见/注意到；realize 偏理解其意义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team achieved its goal.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: achieve 对应 realize a goal 的实现义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The author recognizes the limits of the theory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: recognize 可强调承认某事实。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy helped actualize a long-discussed reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: actualize 更抽象正式，接近“使成为现实”。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report overlooked several important facts.
antonyms.difference_note: overlook 与意识到相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plan failed despite strong support.
antonyms.difference_note: fail 与 realize a goal 的实现义相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The schedule was not realistic.
confusable_words.difference_note: realistic 是形容词；realize 是动词。

word_family.example_sentence: The realization changed her interpretation of the evidence.
The target is ambitious but realizable.

word_family.note: 同 real 词族，语义方向不同。
word_family.example_sentence: As a realist, he doubted the plan would work.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. #4 44320934 a thing that causes disgust or hatred: This bill is an abomination to all mankind. 这条法律让全人类为之反感

headline.core_cn: 令人憎恶的事物；可恶之物
headline.core_en: something regarded with intense disgust or hatred

familiar_bridge.summary: hate 是讨厌/憎恨；abomination 是“让人极其反感的东西”本身。

core.main: abomination 语气很强，常带道德谴责，表示某事物令人厌恶到不可接受。

usage_groups.group_title: 令人强烈憎恶或道德上不可接受的东西
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于强烈批评制度、行为、观念或事物。

examples.sentence: To the reformers, the law was an abomination.
examples.translation: 在改革者看来，这项法律令人深恶痛绝。
examples.teaching_note: an abomination 用于强烈道德批评。

examples.sentence: The critic called the policy a moral abomination.
examples.translation: 评论者称这项政策是道德上令人憎恶的东西。
examples.teaching_note: moral abomination 语气很强。

basic_synonyms.meaning: 令人恐惧或厌恶的事物
basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The camp was a horror that survivors never forgot.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: horror 强调恐惧和震惊；abomination 强调厌恶和谴责。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision was an outrage to many voters.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: outrage 强调激起愤怒。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report documented atrocities committed during the war.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: atrocity 指具体残暴行为。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Censorship was anathema to the writer.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: anathema 常接 to someone，表示某人极其反感。

antonyms.example_sentence: For many citizens, equality remained an ideal.
antonyms.difference_note: ideal 是被追求的正面对象。

antonyms.example_sentence: The rain was a blessing after months of drought.
antonyms.difference_note: blessing 表示有益、值得庆幸的事。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Her abhorrence of cruelty was obvious.
confusable_words.difference_note: abhorrence 是情绪；abomination 是被厌恶的对象。

word_family.example_sentence: The philosopher abominated cruelty.
The prisoners endured abominable conditions.
The plan was abominably executed.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. Whi; Gk; ASHE a view of or attitude toward a situation or event; an opinion: an expression of antiwar sentiments 表达反战态度 n. 情绪 a feeling or emotion: an intense sentiment of horror 强烈的恐惧感

headline.core_en: a feeling, attitude, or opinion, often shared by a group

familiar_bridge.summary: feeling 是感觉；sentiment 更常指一种情绪化态度或公众意见。

core.main: sentiment 可以是个人情感，也可以是群体的态度倾向，如 public sentiment。

usage_groups.group_title: 个人或群体的情感态度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调对某事的情绪化看法，不只是冷静观点。

examples.sentence: Public sentiment turned against the war.
examples.translation: 公众情绪转而反对这场战争。
examples.teaching_note: public sentiment 是社会/政治语境高频搭配。

examples.sentence: The speech appealed to patriotic sentiment.
examples.translation: 这场演讲唤起了爱国情感。
examples.teaching_note: appeal to sentiment 指诉诸情感。

usage_groups.group_title: 一句表达情感或观点的话
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 也可指某人表达出的看法或情绪。

examples.sentence: Many readers shared the sentiment that the ending was too abrupt.
examples.translation: 许多读者也有同样的感受，认为结尾太突然。
examples.teaching_note: share the sentiment 表示认同这种看法/感受。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The song expressed a feeling of loss.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: feeling 更普通；sentiment 可指较成形的情感态度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her opinion changed after the debate.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: opinion 更理性；sentiment 常带情绪色彩。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Public attitudes toward the policy shifted.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: attitude 更广泛，可理性也可情感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The voters showed a disposition toward compromise.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disposition 强调较稳定的倾向。

antonyms.example_sentence: Public indifference allowed the problem to continue.
antonyms.difference_note: indifference 是缺少情感投入。

antonyms.example_sentence: Political apathy worried the reformers.
antonyms.difference_note: apathy 比 indifference 更强调无兴趣、无反应。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The novel avoids sentimentality despite its tragic plot.
confusable_words.difference_note: sentimentality 常指过度情绪化；sentiment 本身不一定贬义。

word_family.example_sentence: The film was sentimental but effective.
The critic accused the poem of sentimentality.
The memoir sentimentalizes a difficult childhood.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 多愁善感的; 伤感的 of or prompted by feelings of tenderness, sadness, or nostalgia: He has sentimental ideas about the past. 他对过去很容易伤感

headline.core_en: strongly influenced by tender, nostalgic, or excessive emotion

familiar_bridge.summary: sentiment 是情感；sentimental 是情感太浓，常带怀旧、柔软，甚至有点过度。

core.main: sentimental 可中性地表示感情浓，也常带贬义：太煽情、太感伤。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容人、物品或时刻带有怀旧、柔软的情绪。

examples.sentence: The old watch has sentimental value for her.
examples.translation: 这块旧表对她有情感价值。
examples.teaching_note: sentimental value 指情感价值，不是经济价值。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 批评电影、小说、演讲等过度诉诸情感。

examples.sentence: The novel becomes sentimental when it asks readers to pity every character.
examples.translation: 这部小说要求读者同情每个人物时，就显得过于煽情。
examples.teaching_note: 文学评论中 sentimental 常带负面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech was emotional but controlled.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: emotional 更宽泛；sentimental 常带怀旧或过度柔情。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The song made him nostalgic for his childhood.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: nostalgic 强调怀念过去。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The play became maudlin in its final scene.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: maudlin 是更贬义的“滥情”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic disliked the film’s lachrymose tone.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lachrymose 更书面，强调催泪或泪水。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her unsentimental analysis impressed the committee.
antonyms.difference_note: unsentimental 是直接对照。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor took a hardheaded approach to the budget.
antonyms.difference_note: hardheaded 强调现实、理性，不被情绪左右。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The judge was sympathetic to the workers’ complaint.
confusable_words.difference_note: sympathetic 是理解或支持他人；sentimental 是情感表达偏浓。

word_family.note: 名词帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: Public sentiment shifted quickly.
The novel avoids cheap sentimentality in its final scene.
The film sentimentalizes poverty.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 可能出错; 容易出错 the tendency to make mistakes or be wrong: the fallibility of human perceptions 人的观感的不可靠性

headline.core_en: the quality of being capable of making mistakes

familiar_bridge.summary: fallible 是会犯错的；fallibility 就是“人或判断本来可能出错”这一点。

core.main: fallibility 常用于讨论人类理性、记忆、判断、制度等并非绝对可靠。

usage_groups.group_title: 人类判断或知识可能出错
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调人、记忆、推理或制度都有犯错的可能。

examples.sentence: The experiment revealed the fallibility of eyewitness memory.
examples.translation: 这个实验揭示了目击者记忆的易错性。
examples.teaching_note: fallibility of memory 是心理学/证据讨论常见表达。

examples.sentence: A good scientist recognizes the fallibility of any single measurement.
examples.translation: 优秀科学家会意识到任何单次测量都可能出错。
examples.teaching_note: recognize fallibility 表示承认不可靠性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The design’s imperfections became clear after testing.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: imperfection 更泛；fallibility 特指会出错。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan’s weakness was its cost.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: weakness 可指任何弱点，fallibility 指判断或人会犯错。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel explores human frailty.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: frailty 更偏人的脆弱性，道德或身体层面都可。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The philosopher discussed the errancy of human judgment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: errancy 更少见、更抽象，强调错误可能性。

antonyms.example_sentence: The doctrine assumes the infallibility of the leader.
antonyms.difference_note: infallibility 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The system aims for reliability, not perfection.
antonyms.difference_note: perfection 是无缺陷状态。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The argument rests on a familiar fallacy.
confusable_words.difference_note: fallacy 是具体错误或谬误；fallibility 是会犯错的性质。

word_family.example_sentence: Human observers are fallible.
No method is infallible.
The claim of infallibility was questioned.

word_family.note: 同源相关，指具体错误逻辑。
word_family.example_sentence: The essay exposes a common fallacy.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. (HAT; (在法律或道德上 ) 迫使 bind or compel (someone), especially legally or morally: The contract obligates the firm to complete the work in six weeks. 合同要求公司在六周内完成工作 。 adj. 【生物 ] 专性的 (只有一种生存方式的 ) restricted to one particularly characteristic mode of life: an obligate parasite 专性寄生虫 %& obligatory /n'bligatari/ adj. 有义务的; 必须的; 强制性的 required by a legal, moral, or other rule; compulsory: /t is not obligatory to answer. 并非一定要回答

headline.core_en: to require someone legally or morally to do something

familiar_bridge.summary: obligation 是义务；obligate 是让某人承担这种义务，常来自法律、合同或道德责任。

core.main: obligate 强调外部规则或道德责任让人必须做某事，常用结构是 obligate someone to do something。

usage_groups.group_title: 法律、合同或责任要求某人做事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某人不是随意选择，而是被规则、承诺或责任要求去做。

examples.sentence: The contract obligates the firm to complete the work by June.
examples.translation: 合同要求公司在六月前完成这项工作。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Doctors are obligated to protect patient confidentiality.
examples.translation: 医生有义务保护病人隐私。
examples.teaching_note: be obligated to 可表示职业或道德义务。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law requires drivers to carry insurance.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: require 更普通；obligate 更强调义务或约束。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm forced the school to close.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: force 可指任何压力；obligate 常来自规则、合同或道德。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence compelled the committee to reconsider.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: compel 语气强，可指强力推动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agreement binds both parties to the terms.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bind 常指法律或协议上的约束。

antonyms.example_sentence: The court released him from the obligation.
antonyms.difference_note: release someone from an obligation 与 obligate 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Small businesses were exempt from the new fee.
antonyms.difference_note: exempt 强调免除某项义务。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The rule obliged companies to disclose the data.
confusable_words.difference_note: oblige 与 obligate 很近；obligate 更正式，常见法律/合同语境。

word_family.example_sentence: The contract created a legal obligation.
Attendance is obligatory for all students.
The agency is obligated to respond.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 烧死; 以烧死的方式作为祭品 kill or offer as a sacrifice, especially by burning: a man who immolated himself as an act of protest 6 4X #

headline.core_en: to kill or destroy, especially by fire, often as a sacrifice

familiar_bridge.summary: mortal 和 death 都和死亡有关；immolate 的画面更极端，是以火焚烧作为祭献或牺牲。

core.main: immolate 是强烈、少见而正式的词，常用于宗教献祭、自焚或象征性牺牲。

usage_groups.group_title: 以火焚烧作为献祭或牺牲
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调通过火焚烧来献祭、杀死或毁灭。

examples.sentence: The protester threatened to immolate himself in front of the building.
examples.translation: 抗议者威胁要在大楼前自焚。
examples.teaching_note: immolate oneself 常指自焚。

examples.sentence: Ancient rituals sometimes involved animals being immolated as sacrifices.
examples.translation: 古代仪式有时包括把动物焚烧作为祭品。
examples.teaching_note: as sacrifices 点出献祭语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fire burned the old barn to the ground.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: burn 最普通；immolate 更正式且常有牺牲/献祭意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The myth describes a king who sacrificed his wealth for peace.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sacrifice 不一定用火；immolate 常带焚烧画面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The documents were incinerated after the trial.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incinerate 强调烧毁成灰，不含献祭意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He self-immolated as an act of protest.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: self-immolate 是固定反身用法。

antonyms.example_sentence: The archive preserves fragile documents.
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 与毁灭/焚烧相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Young athletes try to emulate their heroes.
confusable_words.difference_note: emulate 拼写接近但意思完全不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The report described the act of self-immolation.

word_family.note: 复合名词，常用于抗议语境。
word_family.example_sentence: Self-immolation drew international attention to the protest.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: Vv.ij， 产生共鸣 to have particular meaning or importance for someone; to affect or appeal to someone in a personal or emotional way: These issues resonated with the voters. 这些问题引起了选民的共鸣 。 近义词 ring true

headline.core_en: to produce a strong feeling, meaning, or echo for someone

familiar_bridge.summary: sound 会回响；resonate 把这个声音画面扩展到观点、故事、经历在心里“回响”。

core.main: resonate 可以是真正的声音共振，也更常表示观点或经历引起情感共鸣。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点、故事或经历引起共鸣
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某件事触动人，让人觉得有意义、有关联或有情感回应。

examples.sentence: The candidate’s message resonated with young voters.
examples.translation: 这位候选人的信息引起了年轻选民的共鸣。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: The memoir resonates because it describes fears many readers share.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录能引起共鸣，因为它写出了许多读者共有的恐惧。
examples.teaching_note: 说明为什么会产生共鸣。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 物理或声音语境中，指声音或振动在空间中回荡。

examples.sentence: The singer’s voice resonated through the empty hall.
examples.translation: 歌手的声音在空荡的大厅里回响。
examples.teaching_note: 这是较字面的声音义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her words echoed in his mind for years.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: echo 更字面或比喻回响；resonate 强调产生意义或情感反应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument appealed to moderate voters.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: appeal to 强调吸引力；resonate 强调深层共鸣。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision reverberated throughout the industry.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reverberate 可指声音或影响持续扩散。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The story struck a chord with parents.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 习语，和 resonate with 很接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The joke fell flat with the audience.
antonyms.difference_note: fall flat 表示没有产生预期反应。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The reason for the delay was unclear.
confusable_words.difference_note: reason 与 resonate 拼写/发音不同，语义无关。

word_family.example_sentence: The speech had emotional resonance.
Her resonant voice filled the room.
The instrument uses a wooden resonator.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (活动等所需的 ) 工具; 物品; 配件 an accessory or other item associated with a particular activity or style of living: all the appurtenances of luxurious travel 奢侈旅行的全套装备

headline.core_en: an accessory or subordinate item belonging to something larger

familiar_bridge.summary: accessory 是配件；appurtenance 更正式，强调某物附属于一个主要事物或活动。

core.main: appurtenance 常见于法律、地产或正式说明，指附属于主体的设施、权利或物品。

usage_groups.group_title: 属于某个主体的附属物或设施
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是核心本体，而是随主体一起存在、使用或转让的附属部分。

examples.sentence: The sale included the land, the house, and all appurtenances.
examples.translation: 这次出售包括土地、房屋以及所有附属物。
examples.teaching_note: 法律/地产语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The old workshop still contained the appurtenances of a carpenter’s trade.
examples.translation: 旧工坊里仍有木匠行当的各种附属工具。
examples.teaching_note: 指属于某种活动的配套物品。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The camera comes with several accessories.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: accessory 更常见；appurtenance 更正式、法律味更重。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The vacuum cleaner has a brush attachment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: attachment 强调连接在主体上的部件。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lab served as an adjunct to the main hospital.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: adjunct 可指辅助机构或部分。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The soldier carried the usual accoutrements.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accoutrement 常指服饰、装备、随身配备。

antonyms.example_sentence: The engine is the main component of the machine.
antonyms.difference_note: main component 是主体，不是附属物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The deed included appurtenant rights to the road.
confusable_words.difference_note: appurtenant 是形容词，appurtenance 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The property includes appurtenant rights.

word_family.note: 同源相关，表示“属于/关联”。
word_family.example_sentence: The rule pertains to public schools.

word_family.note: 同源相关，语义走向“有关联”。
word_family.example_sentence: The judge allowed only pertinent questions.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 必要的; 必不可少的; 极为重要的 of vital importance; crucial: Immediate action was imperative. 必须马上采取措施 。 | It is imperative that standards be maintained. 必须要维持标准 。 近义词 ndispensable n. 必须要做的事; 迫在眉睫的事 an essential or urgent thing: She considers it a moral imperative to help people in need. 她认为帮助有需要的人在道德上是必须的 。 | Free movement of labor was an economic imperative. 劳动力自由流动对于经济是必须的 。 n. 必要性; 需求 necessity, need: the terrible imperative of reaching the springs 急切需要到达 RAs | The change came about through a financial imperative. 这一变化是出于财政上的需求

headline.core_cn: 必要的；紧急重要的；必须做的事
headline.core_en: extremely important and requiring action; an urgent duty

familiar_bridge.summary: important 是重要；imperative 更进一步，是“必须做、不能拖”的重要。

core.main: imperative 作形容词表示极其必要；作名词表示必须履行的责任或迫切任务。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于行动、改革、决定等非常重要，不能推迟。

examples.sentence: It is imperative that hospitals maintain reliable emergency systems.
examples.translation: 医院必须维持可靠的急救系统。
examples.teaching_note: It is imperative that... 后常接正式要求。

examples.sentence: Immediate action was imperative after the bridge collapsed.
examples.translation: 桥梁坍塌后，立即行动是必要的。
examples.teaching_note: imperative 强调紧迫重要。

usage_groups.group_title: 必须承担的责任或任务
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作名词时，指强烈的道德、政治或实际责任。

examples.sentence: Reducing child poverty became a moral imperative for the government.
examples.translation: 减少儿童贫困成了政府的道德责任。
examples.teaching_note: moral imperative 是常见抽象搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Sleep is necessary for memory.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: necessary 更普通；imperative 更强，带紧迫感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The patient needed urgent care.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: urgent 强调时间紧迫；imperative 强调必须做。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Trust is crucial to cooperation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: crucial 强调关键性，不一定是命令式必须。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Clean water is indispensable to public health.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indispensable 强调缺了不行。

antonyms.example_sentence: Attendance at the workshop is optional.
antonyms.difference_note: optional 与 imperative 的“必须”相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report removed inessential details.
antonyms.difference_note: inessential 表示不关键。

confusable_words.example_sentence: His imperious tone angered the staff.
confusable_words.difference_note: imperious 形容态度专横；imperative 是必要/命令。

word_family.example_sentence: The order was phrased imperatively.

word_family.note: 同源但意义不同，需区分。
word_family.example_sentence: The manager’s imperious manner alienated employees.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: #474) ¢X n. 曲解; 牌曲 ， 有利于自己的理解 a particular bias, interpretation, or point of view, intended to create a favorable (or sometimes, unfavorable) impression when presented to the public: They claim to report the news with no spin. 他们说自己没有牌曲事实

headline.core_cn: 有倾向的解读；粉饰；旋转
headline.core_en: a biased interpretation meant to influence opinion; to turn around quickly

familiar_bridge.summary: spin 本来是旋转；新闻政治语境里，是把事实“转”成对自己有利的说法。

core.main: GRE/阅读中更重要的是名词义：带偏向的解读、包装或话术。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 为了影响公众看法，把同一事实解释成对自己有利的版本。

examples.sentence: The spokesperson tried to put a positive spin on the disappointing report.
examples.translation: 发言人试图把这份令人失望的报告包装成积极消息。
examples.teaching_note: put a positive spin on 是核心表达。

examples.sentence: The newspaper promised to report the facts with no spin.
examples.translation: 这家报纸承诺不带倾向地报道事实。
examples.teaching_note: with no spin 表示不加粉饰或偏向。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 字面义，指物体转动。

examples.sentence: The wheel began to spin faster as the machine started.
examples.translation: 机器启动后，轮子开始转得更快。
examples.teaching_note: 这是字面旋转义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report showed a clear bias against small firms.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bias 是偏向本身；spin 是带偏向的说法或包装。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article had a political slant.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: slant 与 spin 接近，常指报道或解释角度。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The advertisement was a distortion of the facts.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: distortion 更强调歪曲事实。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The framing of the question shaped the answers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: framing 强调如何设置理解框架，不一定有欺骗意味。

antonyms.example_sentence: The historian valued objectivity over partisanship.
antonyms.difference_note: objectivity 与带偏向的 spin 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mediator’s neutrality helped both sides trust the process.
antonyms.difference_note: neutrality 强调不偏向任何一方。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The Earth rotates once every twenty-four hours.
confusable_words.difference_note: rotate 只是物理旋转，不含政治/媒体包装义。

word_family.example_sentence: The spinning wheel became a symbol of industry.
The popular show produced a spin-off series.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. [总称 ] 商品; 货物 goods to be bought and sold: We sell quality merchandise. 我们卖高级商品

headline.core_en: goods for sale; to promote goods for sale

familiar_bridge.summary: goods 是商品；merchandise 更像商业场景里陈列、销售、推广的货品。

core.main: merchandise 作名词是商品总称；作动词是推销、经营销售商品。

usage_groups.group_title: 商店或公司出售的商品
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指拿来出售的货品，重点是它们作为商品被陈列、库存或交易。

examples.sentence: The store sells outdoor clothing and related merchandise.
examples.translation: 这家商店出售户外服装及相关商品。
examples.teaching_note: merchandise 是商品总称，通常不可数。

examples.sentence: Unsold merchandise filled the warehouse after the holiday season.
examples.translation: 节日季后，仓库里堆满了未售出的商品。
examples.teaching_note: unsold merchandise 是零售语境。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作动词时强调把商品包装、陈列或推广给消费者。

examples.sentence: The company learned how to merchandise its products to younger buyers.
examples.translation: 公司学会了如何向年轻买家推销产品。
examples.teaching_note: 动词义偏商业营销。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The ship carried goods across the river.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: goods 更普通；merchandise 更偏零售和商业语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company introduced three new products.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: products 可指制造出的产品；merchandise 强调拿来卖的货品。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The retailer reduced its inventory before moving.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inventory 是库存清单或库存货品，不等于全部商品概念。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Vendors displayed their wares in the market.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wares 较传统，常指商贩售卖的货品。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The merchant imported spices from abroad.
confusable_words.difference_note: merchant 是卖货的人；merchandise 是商品或推销商品。

word_family.example_sentence: The merchant sold cloth and tools.
Effective merchandising increased sales.
The merchandiser selected items for the spring display.

source.raw_ocr_text: vi. WH; 有志向 ( 获得或达成 ) direct one's hopes or ambitions toward achieving something: Both young men aspire to careers in medicine. 两个年轻人都希望从事医疗工作 。 派生 aspiration /spo'rerfan/ n. 志向; 抱负; 42 a hope or ambition of achieving something: What are your aspirations for the future? 你对未来有什么打算 ?

headline.core_en: to hope or aim for something high or important

familiar_bridge.summary: hope 是希望；aspire 更像“有志于达到更高目标”。

core.main: aspire 常接 to，表达理想、职业、地位或品质上的追求。

usage_groups.group_title: 立志追求目标、职业或理想
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调向较高目标努力，不只是普通想要。
common_patterns: aspire to a career in medicine

examples.sentence: Both students aspire to careers in medicine.
examples.translation: 两个学生都立志从事医学工作。
examples.teaching_note: aspire to careers in... 是这个用法的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The movement aspired to create a fairer society.
examples.translation: 这场运动立志创造一个更公平的社会。
examples.teaching_note: aspire to do something 表达理想目标。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She hopes to study abroad.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hope 更普通；aspire 更强调高目标和志向。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The program aims to reduce waste.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: aim 更中性；aspire 带理想色彩。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team strives to improve every year.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: strive 强调努力过程；aspire 强调抱负目标。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Many immigrants yearned for a safer life.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: yearn 更情感化，未必有明确计划。

antonyms.example_sentence: She refused to settle for a minor role.
antonyms.difference_note: settle for 表示降低期待，与 aspire 的向上追求相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: He renounced his claim to the throne.
antonyms.difference_note: renounce 是正式放弃目标或权利。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Her courage inspired the team.
confusable_words.difference_note: inspire 是激发别人；aspire 是自己追求目标。

word_family.example_sentence: Her aspirations extended beyond local politics.
Aspiring writers need patience.

word_family.meaning: 体现抱负的；令人向往的
word_family.example_sentence: The brand sells an aspirational image.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 超过; vit; 胜过 beat (someone or something) by saying or doing something better: The need for blood donors trumps all other concerns. 对献血者的需要超过一切其他的考虑

headline.core_en: to be more important or powerful than something else; a winning card

familiar_bridge.summary: win 是赢；trump 更像“压过别的考虑”，好像打出一张更大的牌。

core.main: trump 作动词常表示某个理由、需要或原则压倒其他因素。

usage_groups.group_title: 某个因素压倒其他考虑
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一件事的重要性更高，因此超过或盖过其他因素。

examples.sentence: In an emergency, public safety trumps convenience.
examples.translation: 在紧急情况下，公共安全高于便利。
examples.teaching_note: A trumps B 表示 A 比 B 更重要。

examples.sentence: The need for blood donors trumped all other concerns.
examples.translation: 对献血者的需求压倒了其他一切考虑。
examples.teaching_note: 这是这个用法的自然表达。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 名词义来自纸牌，指能赢过其他牌的王牌。

examples.sentence: The new evidence became the lawyer’s trump card.
examples.translation: 新证据成了律师的王牌。
examples.teaching_note: trump card 可比喻决定性优势。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The benefits outweigh the risks.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: outweigh 更常用于权衡利弊；trump 更有压倒感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Experience often beats speed in this job.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: beat 更口语；trump 更正式或比喻。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new rule superseded the old policy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: supersede 强调新事物正式取代旧事物。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court can override a local decision.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: override 强调权力上推翻或压过。

antonyms.example_sentence: Personal preference must yield to safety.
antonyms.difference_note: yield to 表示让步，被更重要因素压过。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The results surpassed expectations.
confusable_words.difference_note: surpass 强调超过标准；trump 强调在冲突中压倒另一因素。

word_family.note: 同形相近但较冷僻，不建议重点记。
word_family.example_sentence: The critic dismissed the decoration as trumpery.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. #5; 偏离 to do something that is different or to be different from what is usual or expected: deviate from the rule 违背规则 | deviate to minor issues 偏离到次要问题上去流下 deviation /di:vi'eifan/ n. 背离;偏离 the action of departing from an established course or accepted standard: a deviation from the truth #3 #2

headline.core_en: to move away from a usual path, standard, or expected pattern

familiar_bridge.summary: via/way 可以联想到路；deviate 是离开原来的路、标准或预期模式。

core.main: deviate 常用于路线、计划、规范、数据或行为偏离原本路径。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某人或某事离开原本路线、规则、计划或正常模式。

examples.sentence: The pilot did not deviate from the planned route.
examples.translation: 飞行员没有偏离预定航线。
examples.teaching_note: deviate from a route/path 是字面或计划义。

examples.sentence: The results deviated sharply from the expected pattern.
examples.translation: 结果明显偏离了预期模式。
examples.teaching_note: deviate from pattern 在科学/分析语境常见。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于社会、伦理或学术语境，表示不符合常规或标准。

examples.sentence: The artist deliberately deviated from academic convention.
examples.translation: 这位艺术家有意背离学院派惯例。
examples.teaching_note: deviate from convention 表示突破常规。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay departs from the usual interpretation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: depart from 与 deviate from 接近，语气较正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The discussion strayed from the main topic.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stray from 更口语，也可指话题跑偏。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The two theories diverge on one crucial point.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: diverge 强调两条路径或观点分开。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The car swerved to avoid the cyclist.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: swerve 更强调突然改变方向。

antonyms.example_sentence: The design conforms to safety standards.
antonyms.difference_note: conform 与偏离标准相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team adhered to the original plan.
antonyms.difference_note: adhere to 强调不偏离计划或原则。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The data showed a small deviation from the mean.
confusable_words.difference_note: deviation 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The deviation from the average was small.

word_family.note: 形容词/名词，常用于行为偏差。
word_family.example_sentence: The study examined deviant behavior.
She showed undeviating loyalty.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 强制性的; 必须要做的 required by law or a rule; obligatory: compulsory legislation 强制性的法律 | compulsory education 义务教育

headline.core_en: required by rule, law, or authority

familiar_bridge.summary: compel 是迫使；compulsory 是“被规则要求，不能自己选择不做”。

core.main: compulsory 强调由法律、制度或学校规定必须执行，不是自愿选择。

usage_groups.group_title: 由规则或法律强制要求
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于课程、服务、考试、措施等必须参加或执行。

examples.sentence: Compulsory education greatly increased literacy.
examples.translation: 义务教育大幅提高了识字率。
examples.teaching_note: compulsory education 表示制度上必须接受的教育。

examples.sentence: Attendance at the safety training is compulsory for all workers.
examples.translation: 所有工人都必须参加安全培训。
examples.teaching_note: is compulsory for someone 表示对某群体强制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A passport is required for international travel.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: required 最普通；compulsory 更强调制度或权威强制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company introduced mandatory training.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mandatory 与 compulsory 很近，常见于正式规则。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: In some countries, voting is obligatory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obligatory 偏义务或规定要求。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: It is imperative that the evidence be preserved.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: imperative 强调紧迫必要，不一定由规则强制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The final workshop is optional.
antonyms.difference_note: optional 与 compulsory 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Participation in the survey is voluntary.
antonyms.difference_note: voluntary 强调由个人选择。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He developed a compulsive habit of checking the door.
confusable_words.difference_note: compulsive 指心理上难以控制，不是制度强制。

word_family.example_sentence: The evidence compelled them to act.
The confession was made under compulsion.

word_family.note: 相关但语义转向心理冲动。
word_family.example_sentence: Compulsive behavior can be difficult to control.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 3%; 3; 放弃 abandon or discard (someone or something that is no longer wanted): They jettisoned plans for a vacation. 他们放弃了休假的计划

headline.core_en: to throw away or abandon something no longer wanted or useful

familiar_bridge.summary: abandon 是放弃；jettison 更有“为了减轻负担或继续前进，把东西扔掉”的画面。

core.main: jettison 最初像船或飞机丢弃货物减重；现在常比喻放弃计划、原则或包袱。

usage_groups.group_title: 丢弃不再有用或太沉重的东西
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 为了减轻负担或摆脱麻烦，把货物、计划、做法或观点抛掉。

examples.sentence: The crew jettisoned cargo to keep the plane in the air.
examples.translation: 机组人员抛掉货物以保持飞机飞行。
examples.teaching_note: 这是接近原始画面的用法。

examples.sentence: The party jettisoned its unpopular policy before the election.
examples.translation: 该党在选举前放弃了不得人心的政策。
examples.teaching_note: 比喻放弃政策或计划。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lab discarded contaminated samples.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: discard 更普通；jettison 常暗示为了前进而抛弃负担。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team abandoned the original plan.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: abandon 可指放弃计划、地点或人；jettison 更像主动甩掉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company shed unprofitable divisions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: shed 可指去掉负担、成本或旧形象。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He renounced his claim to the throne.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renounce 更正式，常指放弃权利、信念或身份。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company retained its most experienced staff.
antonyms.difference_note: retain 是保留，与 jettison 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum preserves rare manuscripts.
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 强调保护不让其消失。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The beach was littered with jetsam after the storm.
confusable_words.difference_note: jetsam 是名词，来自海上抛货；jettison 是动词。

word_family.note: 词源相关，帮助记“抛弃货物”的画面。
word_family.example_sentence: The shore was covered with jetsam.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 幸福; 安康 the state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy: contribute to the well-being of mankind 为人类造福 | physical well-being 健康

headline.core_en: the state of being healthy, comfortable, and satisfied

familiar_bridge.summary: well 是好；being 是存在状态；well-being 就是一个人的整体生活状态好不好。

core.main: well-being 不只是快乐，也包括健康、安全、心理状态和生活质量。

usage_groups.group_title: 人的整体健康和生活状态
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 讨论个人或群体是否健康、安全、满意、能正常生活。

examples.sentence: Regular exercise improves both physical and mental well-being.
examples.translation: 规律运动能改善身心健康。
examples.teaching_note: physical and mental well-being 指身心两个层面的健康状态。

examples.sentence: The policy was judged by its effect on children’s well-being.
examples.translation: 这项政策以其对儿童福祉的影响来评估。
examples.teaching_note: 公共政策语境常用 well-being。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The program aims to improve public welfare.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: welfare 偏社会福利或公共福祉；well-being 更个人和整体状态。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Poor housing can damage health.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: health 偏身体或心理健康；well-being 范围更宽。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Education supports human flourishing.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: flourishing 更强调人的全面发展。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The region enjoyed decades of prosperity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prosperity 偏经济富足，不等于整体福祉。

antonyms.example_sentence: The war caused widespread misery.
antonyms.difference_note: misery 是痛苦状态，与 well-being 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crisis left many families in distress.
antonyms.difference_note: distress 强调身心压力或困境。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Wealth alone does not guarantee happiness.
confusable_words.difference_note: wealth 是金钱资源；well-being 是整体生活状态。

word_family.note: 近义相关，常见于健康管理语境。
word_family.example_sentence: The school opened a wellness center.
The poem reflects on human being.
The children seemed well-adjusted after the move.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (HAE) 有追溯力的; 漳及既往的; 对之前有影响的 (especially of legislation) taking effect from a date in the past: retroactive legislation 7 BHABHA Wik E

headline.core_en: taking effect from a date in the past

familiar_bridge.summary: retro- 有“向后、回到过去”的感觉；retroactive 是现在的规则回头影响过去。

core.main: retroactive 常形容法律、政策、加薪、处罚等对过去某个时间点开始生效。

usage_groups.group_title: 规则或决定追溯到过去生效
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 新规定不是只影响未来，而是回头影响过去已经发生的时期。

examples.sentence: The court rejected the retroactive application of the law.
examples.translation: 法院驳回了该法律的追溯适用。
examples.teaching_note: retroactive application 是法律语境。

examples.sentence: The workers received retroactive pay for the previous six months.
examples.translation: 工人们拿到了过去六个月的追溯工资。
examples.teaching_note: retroactive pay 指补发过去期间应得工资。

basic_synonyms.meaning: 倒填日期的；追溯日期的
basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The agreement was backdated to January.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: backdated 偏文件日期；retroactive 偏法律/政策效果。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum held a retrospective exhibition.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: retrospective 常指回顾过去，不一定改变过去的效力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The constitution forbids ex post facto criminal laws.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 法律术语，尤其指事后追责的法律。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new rule applies only prospectively.
antonyms.difference_note: prospective 与 retroactive 相反，只向未来生效。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Critics called the policy retrograde.
confusable_words.difference_note: retrograde 指倒退或反动，不是追溯生效。

word_family.example_sentence: In retrospect, the decision was risky.
The report offered a retrospective analysis.
The benefit was applied retroactively.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 回避; 避开 ( 问题 、 障得等 ) avoid or circumvent (an obstacle or problem): attempting to bypass the law 试图规避法律 i X44] circumvent

headline.core_en: to go around or avoid an obstacle, rule, or usual process

familiar_bridge.summary: pass 是通过；bypass 是“不从正路过”，而是绕开障碍、程序或问题。

core.main: bypass 可以是实际绕路，也可以是避开规则、程序或困难问题。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不直接通过原本路径，而是从旁边绕过去。

examples.sentence: The new road bypasses the crowded town center.
examples.translation: 新路绕开了拥挤的市中心。
examples.teaching_note: 这是空间路线义。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 为了更快或更方便，不走正常程序，直接越过某一步。

examples.sentence: The company tried to bypass environmental regulations.
examples.translation: 公司试图绕过环保法规。
examples.teaching_note: bypass regulations 常带负面。

examples.sentence: The software bypasses the old login system.
examples.translation: 这个软件绕过了旧登录系统。
examples.teaching_note: 技术语境中指避开某个步骤。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Drivers avoided the flooded road.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: avoid 最普通；bypass 强调绕过原本路径或程序。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We went around the fallen tree.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: go around 更口语、偏空间路线。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The firm circumvented the rule through a loophole.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: circumvent 更正式，常指巧妙规避规定。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The minister sidestepped the question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sidestep 常指回避问题或责任。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report confronts the issue directly.
antonyms.difference_note: confront 是直接面对，不绕开。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team followed the standard procedure.
antonyms.difference_note: follow procedure 与 bypass procedure 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He recovered well after bypass surgery.
confusable_words.difference_note: 这是医学固定用法，不是普通“绕过规则”。

word_family.example_sentence: The city built a bypass to reduce traffic.
The bill passed by a narrow margin.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 人道主义的 concerned with or seeking to promote human welfare: send humanitarian aid 给予人道援助

headline.core_cn: 人道主义的；人道主义者
headline.core_en: concerned with reducing human suffering and improving welfare

familiar_bridge.summary: human 是人；humanitarian 关注人的苦难和援助，不只是“善良”。

core.main: humanitarian 常用于救援、危机、援助、组织或出于人道考虑的行动。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于战争、灾害、贫困或危机中的救助行动。

examples.sentence: The earthquake created an urgent need for humanitarian aid.
examples.translation: 地震造成了对人道援助的迫切需求。
examples.teaching_note: humanitarian aid 是高频表达。

examples.sentence: The organization sent doctors to the humanitarian crisis zone.
examples.translation: 该组织派医生前往人道危机地区。
examples.teaching_note: humanitarian crisis 指大量人受苦的危机。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The shelter demanded more humane treatment of animals.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: humane 强调仁慈对待；humanitarian 强调救助人的苦难。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The foundation supports charitable work.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: charitable 偏慈善捐助，范围较窄。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The university received philanthropic support.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: philanthropic 常指捐助、公益事业。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her motives were genuinely altruistic.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: altruistic 强调动机为他人着想。

antonyms.example_sentence: The prisoners were kept in inhumane conditions.
antonyms.difference_note: inhumane 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy was criticized as cruel.
antonyms.difference_note: cruel 强调造成痛苦或冷酷。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The course takes a humanistic approach to education.
confusable_words.difference_note: humanistic 与人文学科/人文主义有关，不等于救援苦难。

word_family.example_sentence: The disaster tested our humanity.
The law requires humane treatment.
The speech appealed to humanitarianism.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. Il; 自知之明 knowledge or understanding of one's own capabilities, character, feelings, or motivations: Having self-knowledge impelled him to start writing. 自知之明让他开

headline.core_en: understanding of one’s own character, motives, limits, and feelings

familiar_bridge.summary: knowledge 是知识；self-knowledge 是关于自己的知识，知道自己的能力、动机和弱点。

core.main: self-knowledge 强调对自我有清醒认识，而不是只知道事实。

usage_groups.group_title: 认识自己的性格、动机和局限
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于心理、哲学或成长语境，强调人理解自己。

examples.sentence: The memoir is less about fame than about self-knowledge.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录与其说写名声，不如说写自我认识。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象阅读中常与成长、反思相关。

examples.sentence: His lack of self-knowledge made him overestimate his abilities.
examples.translation: 缺乏自知之明让他高估了自己的能力。
examples.teaching_note: lack of self-knowledge 是自然表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Therapy can increase self-awareness.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: self-awareness 更强调觉察；self-knowledge 更强调理解较稳定的自我。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The experience gave her insight into her own fears.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: insight 可针对任何问题；self-knowledge 专指自我。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The poem invites introspection.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: introspection 是自我观察的过程；self-knowledge 是结果。

antonyms.example_sentence: His confidence was really a form of self-deception.
antonyms.difference_note: self-deception 是对自己的误认或欺骗。

antonyms.example_sentence: Ignorance of one’s limits can be dangerous.
antonyms.difference_note: ignorance 可泛指无知，也可与自知相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Praise alone does not build lasting self-esteem.
confusable_words.difference_note: self-esteem 是对自己的评价感受；self-knowledge 是对自己的理解。

word_family.example_sentence: A self-aware leader admits mistakes.
Self-awareness can improve decision-making.
The essay examines self-deception.

source.raw_ocr_text: #74 vt. fH fi; 承担 (责任或负担 ) take on (a burden or responsibility): shoulder all the costs 承担所有的费用 | shoulder the responsibility 承担责任 i 44] assume

headline.core_en: to accept or carry a burden or responsibility

familiar_bridge.summary: shoulder 是肩膀；作动词时，就是把责任或负担“扛到肩上”。

core.main: shoulder 在 GRE/正式语境里常指承担责任、成本、负担。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把困难、责任或费用接过来承受。

examples.sentence: The city had to shoulder the cost of repairing the bridge.
examples.translation: 市政府不得不承担修桥费用。
examples.teaching_note: shoulder the cost 表示承担费用。

examples.sentence: Parents often shoulder much of the burden of care.
examples.translation: 父母常常承担大部分照护负担。
examples.teaching_note: shoulder the burden 是高频表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Taxpayers bear the cost of the program.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bear 更普通；shoulder 更有主动扛起的画面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She took on extra work during the crisis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: take on 口语化，强调接下任务。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He assumed responsibility for the error.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assume responsibility 是正式搭配。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency undertook a major review.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: undertake 强调开始承担一项任务。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official avoided responsibility for the failure.
antonyms.difference_note: avoid responsibility 与 shoulder responsibility 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company shifted the cost to consumers.
antonyms.difference_note: shift the burden/cost 是把负担转给别人。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He shrugged off the criticism.
confusable_words.difference_note: shrug 是耸肩或轻描淡写，不是承担。

word_family.example_sentence: She carried the bag on her shoulder.

word_family.note: 固定复合表达，表示并肩。
word_family.example_sentence: The volunteers worked shoulder-to-shoulder.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 生命的; 生物的 relating to or resulting from living things, especially in their ecological relations: biotic diversity 生物的多样性

headline.core_en: relating to living things, especially in an ecosystem

familiar_bridge.summary: bio- 和 life 有关；biotic 指生态系统中有生命的部分。

core.main: biotic 常和 abiotic 对比：biotic 是生物因素，abiotic 是非生物因素。

usage_groups.group_title: 生态系统中的生物因素
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指由植物、动物、微生物等生命体造成或相关的因素。

examples.sentence: Predation is a biotic factor that affects population size.
examples.translation: 捕食是影响种群数量的生物因素。
examples.teaching_note: biotic factor 指生命体相关因素。

examples.sentence: The study separated biotic influences from abiotic ones such as temperature.
examples.translation: 研究把生物影响和温度等非生物影响区分开来。
examples.teaching_note: biotic 常与 abiotic 对照。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Living organisms respond to their environment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: living 更普通；biotic 更科学、生态学。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The disease has both social and biological causes.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: biological 范围更广；biotic 常限于生态因素。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Organic matter enriches the soil.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: organic 可指含碳/有机体来源，不等于所有生态生物因素。

antonyms.example_sentence: Sunlight and soil chemistry are abiotic factors.
antonyms.difference_note: abiotic 是直接反义，指非生命因素。

confusable_words.difference_note: 这是生态学表达，不是单个物种。

A desert is a harsh biome.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. (overreach oneself) 做力所不能及的事; 自不量力 defeat one's own purpose by trying to do more than is possible: She overreaches herself in her latest book, and her argument is not convincing. 她的新书超过了她的实际能力 ， 书里的东西无法令人信服 。 | The company overreached itself and ran out of money after one year. 公司自不量力 ， 一年后就把钱花光了

headline.core_en: to try to do or take more than is possible, proper, or allowed

familiar_bridge.summary: reach 是伸手够；overreach 是伸得太远，超过能力、权限或合理范围。

core.main: overreach 可指个人/公司自不量力，也可指政府或机构越权。

usage_groups.group_title: 目标超过能力，结果弄巧成拙
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 试图做得太多、扩张太快，最后反而失败。

examples.sentence: The company overreached itself and ran out of money within a year.
examples.translation: 公司扩张过头，一年内就耗尽了资金。
examples.teaching_note: overreach oneself 表示自不量力。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 政府、法院、公司等超出合理或合法权力范围。

examples.sentence: Critics described the surveillance program as government overreach.
examples.translation: 批评者把这项监控计划称为政府越权。
examples.teaching_note: overreach 作名词时常见于政治法律语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The punishment went too far.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: go too far 是日常解释；overreach 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The army overextended its supply lines.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: overextend 强调资源或能力拉得太长。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official exceeded his authority by approving the contract.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 用于权力范围。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new rule encroached on local autonomy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: encroach 强调逐步侵入他人范围或权利。

antonyms.example_sentence: The court restrained the agency’s power.
antonyms.difference_note: restrain 是控制不让其越界。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee tried to stay within its legal bounds.
antonyms.difference_note: 解释性反义表达。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The child could not reach the shelf.
confusable_words.difference_note: reach 是够到；overreach 是过度伸展或越界。

word_family.example_sentence: The court rejected the overreaching claim.
Overextension weakened the empire.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 混合的 of mixed character; composed of mixed parts: the heyday of hybrid cars 混合动力汽车的全盛时期

headline.core_en: something made by combining two different elements

familiar_bridge.summary: mixed 是混合的；hybrid 更像两个不同来源、类型或系统结合成一个新东西。

core.main: hybrid 可作名词或形容词，常用于生物、技术、制度、工作模式等混合体。

usage_groups.group_title: 由两种来源或类型混合而成
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调不同成分结合，不是单一来源。

examples.sentence: The school adopted a hybrid model that combines online and classroom teaching.
examples.translation: 学校采用了线上和课堂教学结合的混合模式。
examples.teaching_note: hybrid model 表示两种模式结合。

examples.sentence: The plant is a hybrid of two wild species.
examples.translation: 这种植物是两个野生物种的杂交种。
examples.teaching_note: 生物语境中 hybrid 可指杂交种。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The results were mixed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mixed 更普通；hybrid 强调不同类型结合成整体。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The combined effort saved time.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: combined 只说明合并；hybrid 强调来源或类别不同。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The material is a composite of plastic and glass.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: composite 更偏由多个部分构成。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The city is an amalgam of cultures.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: amalgam 是名词，强调多种元素融合。

antonyms.example_sentence: The sample was pure copper.
antonyms.difference_note: pure 强调没有混合成分。

antonyms.example_sentence: The community was far from homogeneous.
antonyms.difference_note: homogeneous 强调成分一致。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Hybrid cars use both gasoline and electric power.
confusable_words.difference_note: 这是具体应用，不要把 hybrid 限定成汽车。

word_family.example_sentence: Farmers hybridize plants to improve yield.
Hybridization produced a hardier crop.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 联合; 合并 come together and form one mass or whole: Eleven cities coalesced into one megalopolis. 11 个城市人台并为一个特大城市 。 i 444] collect

headline.core_en: to come together and form one whole

familiar_bridge.summary: together 是在一起；coalesce 更强调多个部分慢慢合成一个整体。

core.main: coalesce 常用于城市、群体、想法、利益等逐渐汇合成一个整体。

usage_groups.group_title: 多个部分合成一个整体
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 原本分散的人、地方、想法或力量逐渐汇合。

examples.sentence: Several small towns coalesced into a single metropolitan area.
examples.translation: 几个小镇合并成了一个大都市区。
examples.teaching_note: coalesce into 表示合成一个整体。

examples.sentence: Over time, the scattered protests coalesced into a national movement.
examples.translation: 随着时间推移，分散的抗议汇聚成全国性运动。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于社会力量或观点汇合。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The two companies merged last year.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: merge 更普通；coalesce 常强调逐渐融合。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis united the community.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unite 强调联合成一体，可有共同目标。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The roads converge near the river.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: converge 强调方向汇合，不一定成为一个整体。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The artist fused jazz and classical styles.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fuse 更强调结合到难以分开的程度。

antonyms.example_sentence: The coalition fragmented after the election.
antonyms.difference_note: fragment 与 coalesce 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crowd dispersed after the speech.
antonyms.difference_note: disperse 强调散开。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The parties formed a coalition.
confusable_words.difference_note: coalition 是合成后的联盟；coalesce 是合并过程。

word_family.example_sentence: The coalescence of local groups strengthened the campaign.
The coalescent movement gained influence.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 遵守传统; 服从规范 behavior in accordance with socially accepted conventions or standards: mindless conformity 不加思考的遵守传统

headline.core_en: behavior that follows accepted rules, standards, or group expectations

familiar_bridge.summary: conform 是遵从；conformity 是遵从规范或群体期待的状态。

core.main: conformity 可中性表示符合标准，也可批评人盲目从众。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调行为、观点或形式与标准或多数人一致。

examples.sentence: The school rewarded conformity more than creativity.
examples.translation: 学校奖励服从规范多于奖励创造力。
examples.teaching_note: conformity 可带批评意味。

examples.sentence: The design was checked for conformity to safety standards.
examples.translation: 这项设计经过检查，以确认符合安全标准。
examples.teaching_note: conformity to standards 是正式用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment studied obedience to authority.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: obedience 强调服从权威；conformity 更强调符合群体或规范。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Compliance with the rule is required.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: compliance 偏规则执行。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic disliked the novel’s conventionality.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conventionality 强调按传统惯例。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime demanded submission.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: submission 语气更强，带放弃抵抗。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her defiance inspired other students.
antonyms.difference_note: defiance 是公开不服从。

antonyms.example_sentence: The artist’s nonconformity made her controversial.
antonyms.difference_note: nonconformity 是直接反义。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The rules created uniformity across schools.
confusable_words.difference_note: uniformity 强调外观或标准一致，不一定涉及服从压力。

word_family.example_sentence: The building must conform to local codes.
He was never a conformist.
The group attracted nonconformists.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. t#iff7A) wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy: an indolent young man 懒惰的年经人 #5044 lethargic 9@ indolence /mdalans/ n. 懒惰; 仍惰;好逸恶劳 avoidance of activity or exertion; laziness: The hot moist air of the tropics spreads a feeling of lethargy and indolence. 热带的湿热空气让人 Fe — FS LEK ZR.

headline.core_en: lazy and avoiding effort

familiar_bridge.summary: lazy 是懒；indolent 更书面，像一种安逸、拖沓、不愿出力的状态。

core.main: indolent 常形容人、生活方式或氛围懒散缺乏活力。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调缺乏行动力，倾向于安逸和拖延。

examples.sentence: The indolent clerk delayed every request until the next day.
examples.translation: 这个懒散的职员把每个请求都拖到第二天。
examples.teaching_note: indolent clerk/student 形容人不愿努力。

examples.sentence: The humid afternoon gave the town an indolent mood.
examples.translation: 潮湿的午后让小镇显得懒洋洋的。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容气氛慵懒。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He was too lazy to finish the task.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lazy 最普通；indolent 更书面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The machines stood idle for weeks.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: idle 可指人或机器闲置；indolent 主要指人/氛围懒散。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The fable warns against slothful habits.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: slothful 更强、更道德化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The heat made everyone lethargic.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lethargic 更偏无精打采，不一定懒。

antonyms.example_sentence: The industrious student revised every draft.
antonyms.difference_note: industrious 是勤奋努力。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her diligent research impressed the professor.
antonyms.difference_note: diligent 强调认真持续用功。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The waiter’s insolent tone angered the customer.
confusable_words.difference_note: insolent 是无礼，不是懒惰。

word_family.example_sentence: His indolence frustrated his colleagues.
He lay indolently in the shade.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 营养品; 滋养物; 食物 a substance that provides nourishment essential for growth and the maintenance of life: The roots transmit moisture and nutrients to the trunk and branches. 根将水分和养料输送到干和枝 。 | a diet rich in nutrients 富有营养的饮食

headline.core_en: a substance that nourishes living things and supports growth

familiar_bridge.summary: nutrition 是营养；nutrient 是具体提供营养的物质。

core.main: nutrient 常用于生物、农业、健康语境，指植物、动物或人体所需养分。

usage_groups.group_title: 维持生长和生命的养分
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指蛋白质、矿物质、氮、磷等能支持生命活动的物质。

examples.sentence: Plants absorb nutrients from the soil through their roots.
examples.translation: 植物通过根从土壤中吸收养分。
examples.teaching_note: soil nutrients 是农业/生态语境。

examples.sentence: A balanced diet provides essential nutrients.
examples.translation: 均衡饮食提供必需营养物。
examples.teaching_note: essential nutrients 是健康语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The refugees needed food and nourishment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: nourishment 更泛；nutrient 指具体营养物质。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The label lists each food substance separately.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 解释性表达，不如 nutrient 科学。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Calcium is an important mineral.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mineral 是营养物的一类。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Protein is a macronutrient.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: macronutrient 是更具体的营养学术语。

antonyms.example_sentence: The water contained a dangerous toxin.
antonyms.difference_note: toxin 损害生命，方向相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The course covers child nutrition.
confusable_words.difference_note: nutrition 是营养过程或学科；nutrient 是具体物质。

word_family.example_sentence: Good nutrition supports health.
Beans are nutritious and inexpensive.
Malnutrition weakened the children.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 宁静的; 平静的 (especially of a place or stretch of water) calm and peaceful, with little movement or activity: a placid world 平静的世界 | a placid temper 平和的性情

headline.core_en: calm, peaceful, and not easily disturbed

familiar_bridge.summary: calm 是平静；placid 更像水面、表情或性格安静、不起波澜。

core.main: placid 常形容湖水、地方、表情或人性格平和。

usage_groups.group_title: 水面、地方或人显得平静
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调没有剧烈活动、焦躁或波动。

examples.sentence: The placid lake reflected the mountains clearly.
examples.translation: 平静的湖面清楚地映出群山。
examples.teaching_note: placid lake 是自然景象高频搭配。

examples.sentence: She answered the accusation with a placid smile.
examples.translation: 她以平静的微笑回应指控。
examples.teaching_note: placid smile/expression 形容情绪不波动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The sea was calm at dawn.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: calm 最普通；placid 更柔和、宁静。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The village was peaceful in the evening.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: peaceful 可指没有冲突；placid 更偏外观或气质平静。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her serene expression reassured the child.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: serene 更有安详、优雅感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The garden was tranquil after the rain.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tranquil 常形容环境安静平和。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crowd grew agitated after the announcement.
antonyms.difference_note: agitated 与 placid 的平静相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plane passed through turbulent air.

She listened placidly.

source.raw_ocr_text: #444). adj. 混乱的 being disordered or mixed up: a contradictory and often confused story 混乱且自相矛盾的故事

headline.core_en: unable to think clearly; unclear or disordered

familiar_bridge.summary: confuse 是使人弄不清；confused 可以说人困惑，也可以说叙述本身混乱。

core.main: confused 有两个方向：人感到困惑，或者故事/论点结构混乱。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不知道发生了什么，或无法理解信息。

examples.sentence: Many readers were confused by the sudden shift in time.
examples.translation: 许多读者被突然的时间转换弄糊涂了。
examples.teaching_note: confused by 表示被某事弄困惑。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 内容组织不清，甚至前后矛盾。

examples.sentence: The witness gave a confused and contradictory account of the accident.
examples.translation: 证人对事故的叙述混乱且自相矛盾。
examples.teaching_note: a confused account 是正式表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She looked puzzled by the question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: puzzled 偏一时不理解；confused 更泛。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were unclear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unclear 可形容信息本身；confused 可形容人或表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sudden noise left everyone bewildered.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bewildered 语气更强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His explanation became incoherent.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incoherent 专指表达逻辑不连贯。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her explanation was clear and brief.
antonyms.difference_note: clear 是主要反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay offered a lucid account of the theory.
antonyms.difference_note: lucid 更书面，强调表达清楚。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The map was confusing.
confusable_words.difference_note: confusing 形容事物让人困惑；confused 形容人困惑或内容混乱。

word_family.example_sentence: The similar names confused the students.
The announcement caused confusion.
The instructions were confusing.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 4b#é; 样子 a person's look or manner, especially one of a particular kind indicating their character or mood: a convict with a threatening mien 4,334 34 hh FEAL

headline.core_en: a person’s look, manner, or bearing

familiar_bridge.summary: manner 是举止；mien 更书面，指一个人整体流露出的神态和气质。

core.main: mien 常用于描写人物外在神态如何透露性格或情绪。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 通过表情、姿态、举止表现出的整体气质。

examples.sentence: Despite the crisis, she maintained a calm and dignified mien.
examples.translation: 尽管身处危机，她仍保持平静而庄重的神态。
examples.teaching_note: mien 常和 calm/dignified/solemn 等形容词搭配。

examples.sentence: Despite the harsh criticism, she maintained a calm and dignified mien.
examples.translation: 尽管受到严厉批评，她仍保持着镇定而庄重的神态。
examples.teaching_note: dignified mien 强调外在神态透露出的气质。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He had a tired look on his face.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: look 更普通；mien 更书面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her manner was polite but distant.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: manner 偏行为方式；mien 是整体神态。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His calm demeanor reassured the audience.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: demeanor 与 mien 接近，更常见。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The general had a commanding bearing.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bearing 强调姿态和气场。

confusable_words.example_sentence: What does this word mean?
confusable_words.difference_note: mien 发音接近 mean，但意思不同。

word_family.example_sentence: Her demeanor remained composed.
His bearing suggested confidence.

source.raw_ocr_text: Vit. (TUTE U ES eA EOS A i) 重新包装; 重新装饰 … 的外表 to package again or anew, specifically to put into a more efficient or attractive form: repackage the products with the aim of achieving greater eye appeal 为使装潢更具视觉吸引力而重新包装产品

headline.core_en: to present something again in a new form or style

familiar_bridge.summary: package 是包装；repackage 是换个包装重新推出，内容可能没变。

core.main: repackage 常用于产品、观点、政策或旧材料换一种形式重新呈现。

usage_groups.group_title: 把旧内容换一种形式重新推出
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点是改变外观、表达方式或市场定位，而不一定改变核心内容。

examples.sentence: The company repackaged the old software as a new productivity tool.
examples.translation: 公司把旧软件重新包装成新的效率工具。
examples.teaching_note: repackage 常暗示换外壳或定位。

examples.sentence: The proposal merely repackaged policies that had already failed.
examples.translation: 这项提案只是把已经失败的政策重新包装了一遍。
examples.teaching_note: 可带批评意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum presented the collection again in a new room.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: present again 是解释性表达；repackage 更强调包装和定位。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company rebranded itself as a technology firm.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rebrand 偏品牌形象；repackage 范围更广。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The author reframed failure as a learning process.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reframe 强调改变理解框架。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The website was revamped for younger users.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: revamp 通常包含实际改动，不只是包装。

antonyms.example_sentence: The editor preserved the original structure.
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 强调不改变呈现方式。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The gift was packaged carefully.
confusable_words.difference_note: package 是第一次包装；repackage 是重新包装。

extension.root_affix: re- 表示“重新”；package 是“包装、组合呈现”。repackage 就是把原有内容换一种包装或说法重新呈现。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: The product comes in a small package.
The packaging made the product look expensive.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 流动性; 移动性 the ability to move or be moved freely and easily: the mobility of a liquid 液体的流动性

headline.core_en: the ability to move or change position freely

familiar_bridge.summary: mobile 是可移动的；mobility 是移动的能力，也可以指社会阶层流动。

core.main: mobility 可指身体移动、交通移动，也常指 social mobility 社会流动。

usage_groups.group_title: 身体、交通或设备的移动能力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调人、机器或系统能否自由移动。

examples.sentence: The injury reduced his mobility for several months.
examples.translation: 这次受伤让他几个月内行动能力下降。
examples.teaching_note: limited/reduced mobility 是身体行动语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 社会阶层或经济地位的流动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指人是否能从一个社会阶层或经济位置移动到另一个位置。

examples.sentence: Education is often seen as a path to upward mobility.
examples.translation: 教育常被视为向上流动的途径。
examples.teaching_note: upward mobility 是社会经济语境高频表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The device tracks movement during sleep.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: movement 是动作或移动本身；mobility 是移动能力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new schedule gives workers more flexibility.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: flexibility 强调可调整；mobility 强调可移动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The robot’s locomotion was surprisingly smooth.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: locomotion 更技术/生物学，指移动方式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel explores the fluidity of identity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fluidity 更抽象，强调边界流动。

antonyms.example_sentence: The cast caused temporary immobility.
antonyms.difference_note: immobility 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: Economic stagnation reduced opportunity.
antonyms.difference_note: stagnation 可与社会/经济流动停滞相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Mobile clinics served remote villages.
confusable_words.difference_note: mobile 是形容词；mobility 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The clinic is mobile and can reach rural towns.
The patient remained immobile after surgery.
The group mobilized volunteers.
Mobilization began after the storm.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 可察觉到的; 看得出来的 (especially of a slight movement or change of state) able to be seen or noticed: The difference is scarcely perceptible to the average reader. 这种差别一般读者几乎察觉不到 。 派生 imperceptible /Impe'septebl/ adj. 无法察觉的; 感觉不到的 impossible to perceive: an almost imperceptible nod 几乎看不出来的点头近义词 indiscernible

headline.core_en: able to be noticed or perceived

familiar_bridge.summary: perceive 是察觉；perceptible 是“能被察觉到”，常指变化虽小但看得出来。

core.main: perceptible 常形容 difference、change、movement、improvement 等微小但可感知的变化。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调变化不一定大，但足够被看到、听到或感受到。

examples.sentence: There was a perceptible change in her tone.
examples.translation: 她的语气有了可察觉的变化。
examples.teaching_note: perceptible change 是自然搭配。

examples.sentence: The difference is barely perceptible to the average reader.
examples.translation: 普通读者几乎察觉不到这个差别。
examples.teaching_note: barely/scarcely perceptible 表示几乎不可察觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: There was a noticeable improvement in his writing.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: noticeable 更普通；perceptible 可指很细微但可察觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The scar was visible in bright light.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: visible 只限视觉；perceptible 可包括听觉、感觉等。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A faint but discernible pattern appeared in the data.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: discernible 更正式，强调能分辨出来。

antonyms.example_sentence: The transition was almost imperceptible.
antonyms.difference_note: imperceptible 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The bacteria were invisible without a microscope.
antonyms.difference_note: invisible 只说视觉不可见。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Her perceptive comments changed the discussion.
confusable_words.difference_note: perceptive 形容人有洞察力；perceptible 形容事物能被察觉。

word_family.example_sentence: Most people could perceive the change.
Public perception shifted quickly.
The movement was imperceptible.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 富有洞察力的 having or showing sensitive insight: a perceptive analysis 深刻的分析 i X44 discerning

headline.core_en: showing quick and accurate understanding

familiar_bridge.summary: perceive 是察觉；perceptive 是人能察觉别人忽略的东西。

core.main: perceptive 常形容人、评论、分析能敏锐看出深层问题。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调能迅速发现细节、动机或意义。

examples.sentence: Her perceptive analysis revealed a flaw in the argument.
examples.translation: 她敏锐的分析揭示了论证中的缺陷。
examples.teaching_note: perceptive analysis/comment 是常见表达。

examples.sentence: A perceptive reader will notice the irony in the final paragraph.
examples.translation: 敏锐的读者会注意到最后一段中的讽刺。
examples.teaching_note: perceptive reader 指能读出深层意味的人。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The review offered insightful comments on the film.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: insightful 强调观点有深度；perceptive 强调观察敏锐。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: An observant child noticed the missing detail.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: observant 更偏注意细节。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Discerning readers noticed the subtle shift in tone.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: discerning 强调判断好坏或差别。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The diplomat made an astute assessment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: astute 常带策略判断或政治智慧。

antonyms.example_sentence: His obtuse response frustrated the teacher.
antonyms.difference_note: obtuse 与 perceptive 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: An unobservant reader might miss the clue.
antonyms.difference_note: unobservant 是不注意细节。

confusable_words.example_sentence: There was a perceptible change in temperature.
confusable_words.difference_note: perceptible 形容事物可被察觉；perceptive 形容人敏锐。

word_family.example_sentence: She perceived a hidden risk.
The campaign changed public perception.
His perceptiveness made him a good critic.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj，[ 接动词不定式 ] 惯常于 … 的; 有 … 习惯的 (of a person) in the habit of doing something; accustomed: He was wont to arise at 5. 他以前习惯五点起床

headline.core_en: accustomed or inclined to do something

familiar_bridge.summary: habit 是习惯；wont 是很书面的“惯常如此”，常见结构 be wont to do。

core.main: wont 多见于正式或文学语境，表示某人习惯做某事。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调长期惯常行为，不是一次性选择。

examples.sentence: He was wont to rise before dawn.
examples.translation: 他习惯在黎明前起床。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: As was her wont, she answered criticism with silence.
examples.translation: 她照例以沉默回应批评。
examples.teaching_note: as was her wont 表示“如她惯常那样”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She was accustomed to long hours of work.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: accustomed 更常见；wont 更文学/正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He used to walk to school.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: used to 更口语；wont 更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She is inclined to question easy answers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inclined 表示倾向，不一定是长期习惯。

antonyms.example_sentence: He was unaccustomed to public attention.
antonyms.difference_note: unaccustomed 是不习惯。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She won’t attend the meeting.
confusable_words.difference_note: wont 没有撇号，是“习惯于”；won’t 是 will not。
