source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. DEIR; 不愿接受 hesitate or be unwilling to accept an idea or undertaking: The public balked at the President's new tax plan. 公众不愿接受总统的新税收计划

headline.core_cn: 畏缩；拒绝继续；不愿接受
headline.core_en: to stop short or refuse to proceed

familiar_bridge.summary: refuse 是拒绝；balk 更像到某一步突然犹豫、退缩、不肯继续。

core.main: balk 常接 at，表示面对计划、价格、要求时不愿接受。

usage_groups.group_title: 面对要求、价格或计划时退缩
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是平静不同意，而是遇到某个阻碍后不愿继续。

examples.sentence: Many voters balked at the cost of the new stadium.
examples.translation: 许多选民因为新体育馆的费用而退缩。
examples.teaching_note: balk at the cost 是自然表达。

examples.sentence: The committee balked at approving the plan without more evidence.
examples.translation: 委员会不愿在缺少更多证据的情况下批准计划。
examples.teaching_note: balk at doing something 表示不愿继续。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She hesitated before signing the contract.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hesitate 强调犹豫；balk 更强调因此停下或拒绝。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He refused to answer the question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: refuse 是明确拒绝；balk 常因顾虑而不愿接受。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The senator demurred at the proposed wording.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: demur 更正式，偏提出反对。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She recoiled at the thought of lying.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: recoil 更强调本能反感或害怕。

antonyms.example_sentence: The board accepted the proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: accept 与 balk at 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: After the vote, the project proceeded.
antonyms.difference_note: proceed 是继续向前。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The bulk of the work is done.
confusable_words.difference_note: bulk 拼写接近但无关。

word_family.example_sentence: The balky engine refused to start.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 编码 convert into a coded form: a technology that encodes images 给图像编码的技术

headline.core_cn: 编码；把信息转换成代码
headline.core_en: to convert information into a coded form

familiar_bridge.summary: code 是代码；encode 是把信息转成某种代码或可储存形式。

core.main: encode 常用于计算机、语言、DNA、记忆等信息转换。

usage_groups.group_title: 把信息转成代码或符号
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让信息以另一种系统储存、传输或表达。

examples.sentence: The program encodes the message before sending it.
examples.translation: 程序在发送前对信息进行编码。
examples.teaching_note: encode data/message 是技术语境。

examples.sentence: DNA encodes instructions for building proteins.
examples.translation: DNA 编码了合成蛋白质的指令。
examples.teaching_note: 生物语境中 encode 也很常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The software codes the data automatically.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: code 可作动词；encode 更强调转成代码形式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The device converts sound into digital signals.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: convert 更宽泛，encode 是转换成编码。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The app encrypts private messages.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: encrypt 是为了保密而编码。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The researcher transcribed the interview.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: transcribe 是从一种记录形式转成文字，不一定编码。

antonyms.example_sentence: The receiver decoded the signal.
antonyms.difference_note: decode 是反向过程。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The file was encoded for storage and encrypted for security.
confusable_words.difference_note: encode 不一定保密；encrypt 重点是安全。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: The code stores the instructions.
The encoder compressed the video.
The brain decodes visual signals.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (来自外部的 ) 动机; 激励; 刺激 something that incites or has a tendency to incite to determination or action: There is no incentive for customers to conserve water. 顾客没有节约用水的动力

headline.core_en: something that encourages a person to act

familiar_bridge.summary: motivation 是动机；incentive 常是外部给出的奖励、好处或压力。

core.main: incentive 用来解释为什么人愿意采取某种行动。

usage_groups.group_title: 促使人行动的奖励或诱因
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某个利益、奖励或条件让人更愿意做某事。

examples.sentence: Tax breaks gave companies an incentive to invest in clean energy.
examples.translation: 税收优惠给了公司投资清洁能源的激励。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: There is little incentive for customers to conserve water when prices are low.
examples.translation: 水价低时，消费者几乎没有节水动力。
examples.teaching_note: incentive explains behavior。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Money was not her only motivation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: motivation 可来自内心；incentive 常是外部刺激。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He had a good reason to leave.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reason 更宽泛；incentive 强调促进行动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The bonus served as an inducement to stay.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inducement 更正式，常指外部诱导。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy acted as a stimulus to growth.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stimulus 更偏刺激或促进因素。

antonyms.example_sentence: High fees can be a deterrent to application.
antonyms.difference_note: deterrent 阻止行动，与 incentive 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The speech incited violence.
confusable_words.difference_note: incite 是动词且常负面；incentive 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The policy incentivizes recycling.
High taxes created a disincentive to invest.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 机灵的; 敏锐的; 精明的 marked by clever discerning awareness and hardheaded acumen: a shrewd guess 机灵的猜测 | a shrewd observer 敏锐的观察家近义词 canny

headline.core_en: showing sharp practical judgment

familiar_bridge.summary: clever 是聪明；shrewd 是能看清利益和局势的精明。

core.main: shrewd 常形容判断、观察、交易、商人等实际而敏锐。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 能快速看出局势、利益或风险，做出聪明决定。

examples.sentence: Her shrewd assessment of the market saved the company money.
examples.translation: 她对市场的精明判断为公司省了钱。
examples.teaching_note: shrewd assessment 强调实际判断力。

examples.sentence: The negotiator made a shrewd concession at the right moment.
examples.translation: 谈判者在恰当时机做出了精明的让步。
examples.teaching_note: 可带褒义，但有精算感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The child found a clever solution.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: clever 更宽泛；shrewd 更实际、懂得利益。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was a smart move.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: smart 更口语；shrewd 更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The analyst made an astute prediction.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: astute 和 shrewd 接近，偏判断敏锐。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The king relied on sagacious advisers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sagacious 更正式，带智慧深度。

antonyms.example_sentence: It was naive to trust the promise without evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: naive 缺少实际判断。

antonyms.example_sentence: Gullible investors believed the false claim.
antonyms.difference_note: gullible 是容易被骗，与 shrewd 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The old comedy calls the heroine a shrew.
confusable_words.difference_note: shrew 与 shrewd 词形相关但现代意义不同。

word_family.example_sentence: Her shrewdness impressed the board.
He shrewdly avoided the risky investment.

headline.core_en: a confused mixture of different things

familiar_bridge.summary: mixture 是混合物；hodgepodge 是没有章法、拼在一起的大杂烩。

core.main: hodgepodge 常带批评，强调元素杂乱、不协调。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不同东西混在一起，但缺少清晰结构或统一性。

examples.sentence: The proposal was a hodgepodge of old ideas and new slogans.
examples.translation: 这个提案是旧想法和新口号拼成的大杂烩。
examples.teaching_note: a hodgepodge of ideas/styles/rules 是自然结构。

examples.sentence: His speech became a hodgepodge of anecdotes, statistics, and complaints.
examples.translation: 他的演讲变成了轶事、数据和抱怨的大杂烩。
examples.teaching_note: 强调混乱、缺少组织。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The soup was a mixture of vegetables and spices.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mixture 中性；hodgepodge 带杂乱批评。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The drawer contained a jumble of old cables.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: jumble 强调乱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The book is a miscellany of essays and letters.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: miscellany 可中性，指多种材料集合。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy became a mishmash of compromises.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mishmash 口语感更强。

antonyms.example_sentence: The editor turned the fragments into a coherent whole.
antonyms.difference_note: coherent whole 与杂乱拼凑相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The room was a hotchpotch of furniture styles.

headline.core_en: extremely serious, urgent, or terrible

familiar_bridge.summary: serious 是严重；dire 是严重到危急、可怕、必须马上面对。

core.main: dire 常形容情况、后果、警告、需求或贫困等极端严重状态。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调危险、紧急、糟糕到令人担忧。

examples.sentence: The region faces dire consequences if the drought continues.
examples.translation: 如果干旱持续，该地区将面临极严重后果。
examples.teaching_note: dire consequences 是高频表达。

examples.sentence: Hospitals were in dire need of basic supplies.
examples.translation: 医院急需基本物资。
examples.teaching_note: in dire need of 表示极度需要。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mistake had serious consequences.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: serious 更普通；dire 更极端、更紧急。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm caused terrible damage.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: terrible 更口语；dire 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report raised grave concerns.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: grave 语气正式，接近 dire。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The flood had calamitous effects on the region.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: calamitous 强调灾难性后果。

antonyms.example_sentence: The injury was minor.
antonyms.difference_note: minor 与 dire 的严重程度相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The doctor gave a reassuring report.
antonyms.difference_note: reassuring 与 dire warning/news 相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He wore a dour expression.
confusable_words.difference_note: dour 形容人或表情阴沉，不是情况危急。

word_family.example_sentence: The village was direly short of water.
The direst predictions did not come true.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 县体的; 明确的 specific; definite: J haven't got any concrete evidence. 我没有具体的证据 。 i 3034] conclusive

headline.core_en: specific and real; a hard building material

familiar_bridge.summary: abstract 是抽象；concrete 是能落到事实、证据、例子上的具体。

core.main: concrete 在 GRE 中常是“具体明确”，另有名词“混凝土”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是抽象空话，而是有证据、例子或明确细节。

examples.sentence: The committee asked for concrete evidence before changing the rule.
examples.translation: 委员会要求有具体证据后才改变规则。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配很常见。

examples.sentence: The proposal needs concrete steps, not vague promises.
examples.translation: 这项提案需要具体步骤，而不是含糊承诺。
examples.teaching_note: concrete 与 vague/abstract 对比。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作名词时指建筑材料。

examples.sentence: The bridge was built with reinforced concrete.
examples.translation: 这座桥用钢筋混凝土建成。
examples.teaching_note: 名词义和抽象/具体义不同。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report gives specific recommendations.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: specific 强调明确细节；concrete 强调实在、有证据或可操作。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We need a definite answer by Friday.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: definite 强调不含糊。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform produced tangible benefits.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tangible 强调可以感知或实际看到。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The talks made substantive progress.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: substantive 强调有实际内容。

antonyms.example_sentence: The argument remained too abstract.
antonyms.difference_note: abstract 是最直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were vague.
antonyms.difference_note: vague 强调不明确。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The path was made of concrete.
confusable_words.difference_note: 名词是建筑材料，形容词是具体明确。

word_family.example_sentence: The plan explains concretely how funds will be used.
The essay lacked concreteness.
The concept was too abstract for young children.

headline.core_en: to tell something in detail; to count again

familiar_bridge.summary: count 是“数”；recount 既可以是“重新数一遍”，也可以是把经历或事件“讲述出来”。

core.main: recount 的重点是有顺序地把事情说出来；在选举或统计语境中，它也可以表示重新计数。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把过去发生的事按细节讲出来，常带有叙事顺序。

examples.sentence: She recounted the events of the night in careful detail.
examples.translation: 她仔细讲述了那天晚上发生的事。
examples.teaching_note: recount the events 强调按细节叙述经过。

examples.sentence: The memoir recounts his years in exile.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录讲述了他的流亡岁月。
examples.teaching_note: recount 常用于回忆录、报道或历史叙述。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在选举、统计或争议结果中，再数一遍以确认数字。

examples.sentence: The candidate demanded a recount after the narrow election loss.
examples.translation: 这名候选人在以微弱差距落败后要求重新计票。
examples.teaching_note: vote recount 是政治新闻中常见用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He told the story plainly.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: tell 最普通；recount 更强调有条理、有细节地讲述。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher asked students to retell the legend in their own words.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: retell 是再次讲述；recount 可以是首次详细叙述。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The documentary narrates the rise of the movement.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: narrate 更正式，常指叙事或旁白。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He related the incident to the investigators.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: relate 在正式语境中可表示讲述经历或事件。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness withheld key details from the report.
antonyms.difference_note: withhold 强调不提供信息，与 recount 的讲出细节相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The clerk counted the ballots twice.
confusable_words.difference_note: count 只是计数；recount 可表示重新计数，也可表示讲述。

word_family.note: 和 recount 共享“讲述事情经过”的语义场。
word_family.example_sentence: Her account of the accident was consistent.

word_family.note: recount 的名词/动名词形式。
word_family.example_sentence: His recounting of the trip was vivid.

related_words.note: 与 count 的“计算”义相关，但不是 recount 的近义词。
related_words.example_sentence: The counter recorded each visitor automatically.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 同时的; 同时发生的; 同时存在的 happening at the same time: the simultaneous failure of all the lifts in a building 一由大楼全部电梯的同时故障 | The audience burst into simultaneous applause. RAF FR. RE simultaneity /simalta'nioty/ n. 同时; 同时发生 the quality or state or an instance of being simultaneous: the simultaneity of these events 事件的同时性

headline.core_en: happening at the same time

familiar_bridge.summary: similar time 不是这个词的来源，但可以帮助记：simultaneous 就是两件事在同一时间发生。

core.main: simultaneous 强调两个或多个动作、事件或过程没有先后顺序，而是在同一时间进行。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调多个动作或事件在时间上重合，没有明显先后。

examples.sentence: The two experiments produced simultaneous changes in temperature and pressure.
examples.translation: 两个实验同时引起了温度和压力的变化。
examples.teaching_note: simultaneous changes 表示变化同步发生。

examples.sentence: The city faced simultaneous power failures in several districts.
examples.translation: 这座城市几个区同时发生停电。
examples.teaching_note: simultaneous 强调多个地点或事件并发。

usage_groups.group_title: 同步进行的翻译或传播
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调翻译、传播或播放几乎同步完成。

examples.sentence: The speech was delivered with simultaneous translation into three languages.
examples.translation: 这场演讲配有三种语言的同声传译。
examples.teaching_note: simultaneous translation 是固定自然表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The alarms went off at the same time.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 最日常表达；simultaneous 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report examines concurrent changes in income and education.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: concurrent 常用于正式分析，强调并行发生。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The system allows synchronous communication between users.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: synchronous 更偏技术或过程同步。

antonyms.example_sentence: The tasks must be completed in sequential order.
antonyms.difference_note: sequential 强调一个接一个。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy changed over successive administrations.
antonyms.difference_note: successive 强调前后相继。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The two methods produced similar results.
confusable_words.difference_note: similar 是“相像”，不是“同时”。

word_family.example_sentence: Several factors operated simultaneously.
The study questioned the simultaneity of the two events.

related_words.note: 和“同时/同步”语义相关。
related_words.example_sentence: The devices synchronize automatically.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ， 宽宏大量 generosity: the magnanimity of soul 心灵的高尚 | magnanimity in politics 政治上的宽宏大量 if N44] benevolence

headline.core_en: generosity and nobility of spirit, especially toward someone weaker or defeated

familiar_bridge.summary: magnanimous 是“宽宏大量的”；magnanimity 是这种气度本身。

core.main: magnanimity 不是普通“慷慨给钱”，而是面对对手、失败者或冒犯自己的人时表现出的气量。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一个人在占优势时仍不报复、不羞辱对方，而表现出高尚气度。

examples.sentence: After winning the election, she showed magnanimity toward her former opponents.
examples.translation: 赢得选举后，她对昔日对手表现出宽宏大量。
examples.teaching_note: magnanimity toward opponents 是典型语境。

examples.sentence: The general’s magnanimity in victory helped prevent further violence.
examples.translation: 将军胜利后的宽宏大量帮助避免了进一步暴力。
examples.teaching_note: in victory 突出有优势时仍不报复。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His generosity helped many young artists.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: generosity 可指物质或精神慷慨；magnanimity 更强调气度。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her nobility was evident in the way she forgave her critics.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: nobility 强调品格高尚。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ruler was praised for his benevolence toward the poor.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: benevolence 偏仁慈善意；magnanimity 更常在冲突或胜负后出现。

antonyms.example_sentence: His pettiness made every disagreement feel personal.
antonyms.difference_note: pettiness 是气量小。

antonyms.example_sentence: The punishment reflected vindictiveness rather than justice.
antonyms.difference_note: vindictiveness 强调想报复，与 magnanimity 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The foundation supports local charities.
confusable_words.difference_note: charity 主要是慈善行为，不等同于面对对手时的宽宏。

word_family.example_sentence: A magnanimous leader forgave the insult.
He responded magnanimously to the criticism.

related_words.note: animus 和“精神/心意”相关，但现代常指敌意。
related_words.example_sentence: The remark revealed no personal animus.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: 3474) 4X ad. (程度 ) 巨大的; 深远的; (情绪 ) 极为强烈的 (of a state, quality, or emotion) very great or intense: profound feelings of disquiet 强烈的不安 | profound differences 深刻的分歧

headline.core_en: very deep, serious, or far-reaching

familiar_bridge.summary: deep 可以是物理上的深；profound 更常说思想、影响、变化或情感“深”。

core.main: profound 可以形容思想很深刻，也可以形容影响很深远、情感很强烈。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调思想、问题或理解有深度，不停留在表面。

examples.sentence: Her essay offers a profound insight into the causes of social isolation.
examples.translation: 她的文章对社会孤立的原因提出了深刻见解。
examples.teaching_note: profound insight 是常见学术搭配。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调影响进入更深层结构，持续改变后续发展。

examples.sentence: The discovery had a profound impact on modern biology.
examples.translation: 这一发现对现代生物学产生了深远影响。
examples.teaching_note: profound impact 强调影响重大而持久。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调情绪或氛围的程度很深、很沉重。

examples.sentence: A profound silence followed the announcement.
examples.translation: 公告之后是一阵深深的沉默。
examples.teaching_note: profound silence 表示沉默的程度和气氛很重。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel raises deep questions about memory.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: deep 更普通；profound 更正式、程度更强。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The error had serious consequences.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: serious 强调严重性；profound 强调深度或深远影响。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform had far-reaching consequences for education.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: far-reaching 专指影响范围广、持续久。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic offered a penetrating analysis of the poem.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: penetrating 常形容分析或洞察敏锐。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report gave only a superficial account of the crisis.
antonyms.difference_note: superficial 是思想或处理层面浅。

antonyms.example_sentence: His explanation was too shallow to be convincing.
antonyms.difference_note: shallow 更日常，可批评理解不深。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She offered profuse apologies after the mistake.
confusable_words.difference_note: profuse 是“大量的”，不是“深刻的”。

word_family.example_sentence: The loss profoundly affected the community.
The lecture had moments of real profundity.

related_words.note: 基础名词，可帮助理解 profound 的核心。
related_words.example_sentence: The argument lacked depth.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 深刻; 深奥 deep insight; great depth of knowledge or thought: the profundity of the message 信息的深奥

headline.core_en: great depth of thought, feeling, or meaning

familiar_bridge.summary: profound 是“深刻的”；profundity 是“深刻性”或“深度”。

core.main: profundity 常评价思想、作品或话语是否有真正深度，也可带一点“深奥难懂”的感觉。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调作品、观点或话语不只是表面聪明，而是真的有思想深度。

examples.sentence: The book’s apparent simplicity hides great profundity.
examples.translation: 这本书表面简单，却隐藏着很深的思想。
examples.teaching_note: profundity 强调作品或思想的深度。

examples.sentence: Critics admired the profundity of her final speech.
examples.translation: 评论家赞赏她最后演讲的深刻性。
examples.teaching_note: the profundity of... 是自然结构。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某些话看起来深奥，但可能只是装出深刻感。

examples.sentence: The slogan had an air of profundity, but it said very little.
examples.translation: 这句口号看似深刻，其实没说什么。
examples.teaching_note: an air of profundity 可带讽刺意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay has more depth than it first appears.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: depth 最基础；profundity 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her insight into human motives was impressive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: insight 是具体洞见；profundity 是整体深刻性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The story contains a quiet wisdom about aging.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wisdom 偏人生智慧，不等同于思想深度。

antonyms.example_sentence: The review criticized the superficiality of the argument.
antonyms.difference_note: superficiality 是缺乏深度。

antonyms.example_sentence: The shallowness of the analysis disappointed readers.
antonyms.difference_note: shallowness 更日常地批评不够深入。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The garden had a profusion of flowers.
confusable_words.difference_note: profusion 是数量多，和 profundity 的“深度”不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The speech had a profound effect.
The decision profoundly changed the field.

related_words.note: 帮助把 profundity 理解成抽象层面的深度。
related_words.example_sentence: Depth matters more than length in this essay.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

Needed genuine relation examples and a useful false-profundity usage.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 累积的; 逐渐积累的 increasing or increased in quantity, degree, or force by successive additions: The cumulative effects of many illnesses made him a weak man. 多种疾病长期的折磨使他身体虚弱

headline.core_en: increasing by adding one thing after another

familiar_bridge.summary: accumulate 是“积累”；cumulative 形容影响、证据或结果一点点加起来。

core.main: cumulative 强调单次影响可能不大，但多次叠加后形成明显结果。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调结果不是一次造成的，而是多次因素慢慢叠加出来的。

examples.sentence: The cumulative effect of small daily choices can be enormous.
examples.translation: 每天小选择的累积影响可能非常巨大。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配很常见。

examples.sentence: Years of drought caused cumulative damage to the soil.
examples.translation: 多年干旱对土壤造成了累积性损害。
examples.teaching_note: damage 可以被多次事件逐渐累积。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调证据、知识或学习内容不断增加，最后形成更强判断。

examples.sentence: Cumulative evidence suggests that the drug is effective.
examples.translation: 不断积累的证据表明这种药是有效的。
examples.teaching_note: cumulative evidence 表示证据一点点累积成判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The accumulated evidence was hard to ignore.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: accumulated 更直接表示已经积累；cumulative 强调累积过程和效应。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The aggregate cost was higher than expected.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: aggregate 强调整体合计，不一定强调逐步累积。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy produced incremental improvements over time.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incremental 强调一步步小幅增加。

antonyms.example_sentence: An isolated error does not prove a larger pattern.
antonyms.difference_note: isolated 强调单个事件，不是累积形成。

antonyms.example_sentence: A single event rarely explains such a broad change.
antonyms.difference_note: single 和 cumulative 的多次叠加相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The decision reflected collective responsibility.
confusable_words.difference_note: collective 强调群体共同，不等于累积。

word_family.example_sentence: Dust accumulated on the shelves.
The accumulation of evidence changed the debate.
These small losses cumulatively weakened the firm.

related_words.note: 词源上与“堆积”有关，但这里只作轻量联想。
related_words.example_sentence: Cumulus clouds gathered in the afternoon.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. ”华丽的辞藻; 浮夸的言辞 language designed to have a persuasive or impressive effect on its audience, but often regarded as lacking in sincerity or meaningful content: All we have from the Opposition is empty rhetoric. 反对党能说的都是空话 。 近头词 eloquence

headline.core_cn: 修辞；说服性言辞；空洞漂亮话
headline.core_en: language used to persuade, impress, or influence people

familiar_bridge.summary: rhetorical question 是“修辞性问题”；rhetoric 是更大的概念：用语言去说服、影响或包装观点。

core.main: rhetoric 可以是中性的“修辞/说服术”，也可以带贬义，指漂亮但空洞的政治话语。

usage_groups.group_title: 用语言说服或影响别人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于演讲、政治、辩论和文章中，关注语言如何塑造观点。

examples.sentence: The candidate’s rhetoric appealed to voters who wanted rapid change.
examples.translation: 这位候选人的言辞打动了希望快速变革的选民。
examples.teaching_note: political rhetoric 常指政治说服话语。

examples.sentence: The essay analyzes the rhetoric of scientific objectivity.
examples.translation: 这篇文章分析了科学客观性的话语修辞。
examples.teaching_note: rhetoric 可用于学术分析语言如何制造权威感。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常与 reality、action、policy 对比，表示说得好听但未必有实质。

examples.sentence: The promise sounded like mere rhetoric without a concrete plan.
examples.translation: 这个承诺没有具体计划，听起来只是空话。
examples.teaching_note: mere rhetoric 带贬义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His speech focused on economic reform.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: speech 是具体讲话；rhetoric 更关注说服方式或话语风格。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader was admired for his powerful oratory.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: oratory 更偏演讲技巧。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her eloquence made the argument memorable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: eloquence 偏表达流畅有感染力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article examines public discourse about climate change.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: discourse 更学术，指某领域的话语体系。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report used plain speech rather than slogans.
antonyms.difference_note: plain speech 与华丽或策略化修辞相对。

antonyms.example_sentence: The public wanted action, not rhetoric.
antonyms.difference_note: 在“空谈 vs 行动”语境中 action 是对照词。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The posters were designed as wartime propaganda.
confusable_words.difference_note: propaganda 通常更强烈地指有目的的宣传操控；rhetoric 可中性也可贬义。

word_family.example_sentence: He asked a rhetorical question.
The phrase works rhetorically but not logically.

word_family.note: 指研究或擅长修辞的人。
word_family.example_sentence: The rhetorician studied ancient speeches.

related_words.note: 与 rhetoric 的演说语境相关。
related_words.example_sentence: The orator held the crowd’s attention.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 尊敬 ， 敬意 deferential respect: They paid obeisance to the king. th 1K BME E.

headline.core_en: a gesture or attitude of deep respect or submission

familiar_bridge.summary: obedient 是“顺从的”；obeisance 更像一种正式的致敬姿态或恭顺态度。

core.main: obeisance 可以指身体上的鞠躬行礼，也可以指对权威、传统或权力的象征性服从。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于礼仪、宗教、宫廷或正式场合，表示用动作表达尊敬。

examples.sentence: The visitors bowed in obeisance before the shrine.
examples.translation: 访客在神龛前鞠躬致敬。
examples.teaching_note: bow in obeisance 表示正式行礼。

usage_groups.group_title: 对权威或传统表示顺从
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在抽象语境中，表示对权威、传统或制度的恭顺态度。

examples.sentence: The ceremony paid obeisance to traditions that the reformers had hoped to change.
examples.translation: 这场仪式向改革者原本希望改变的传统表示了敬意。
examples.teaching_note: pay obeisance to tradition 可带有顺从传统的意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The students showed respect for their teacher.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: respect 最普通；obeisance 更正式，常带动作或服从意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official spoke with deference to the court.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deference 强调对地位或权威的尊重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The film pays homage to classic cinema.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: homage 偏纪念或致敬；obeisance 可更恭顺。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The treaty demanded submission to imperial rule.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: submission 服从意味更强，不一定含尊敬。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her defiance angered the authorities.
antonyms.difference_note: defiance 是公开反抗。

antonyms.example_sentence: The gesture was interpreted as disrespect.
antonyms.difference_note: disrespect 是缺乏敬意。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The school demanded strict obedience.
confusable_words.difference_note: obedience 指遵守命令；obeisance 更强调行礼或恭顺姿态。

word_family.example_sentence: The ambassador made an obeisant gesture.
He bowed obeisantly before the ruler.

related_words.note: 基础熟词，帮助理解顺从义。
related_words.example_sentence: Citizens are expected to obey the law.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 辛苦的; 费时费力的 (especially of a task, process, or journey) requiring considerable effort and time: years of laborious training 多年的艰苦训练

headline.core_en: requiring a lot of time, effort, and care

familiar_bridge.summary: labor 是“劳动”；laborious 形容一件事做起来很费劲、很耗时间。

core.main: laborious 常形容 process、task、work、method 等，重点是费力、缓慢、需要耐心。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于需要大量步骤、体力或脑力的工作。

examples.sentence: Restoring the old manuscript was a laborious process.
examples.translation: 修复这份旧手稿是一个费时费力的过程。
examples.teaching_note: laborious process 强调步骤繁多、耗费精力。

examples.sentence: The team completed the laborious task of checking every record by hand.
examples.translation: 团队完成了逐条手工核对记录这项艰苦任务。
examples.teaching_note: laborious task 适合描述细致但费劲的工作。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调文字或解释读起来费劲，好像作者表达得很吃力。

examples.sentence: The argument was buried in laborious prose.
examples.translation: 这个论点被艰涩费力的文字掩盖了。
examples.teaching_note: laborious prose 批评文字读起来费劲。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was hard work, but they finished it.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hard 最普通；laborious 更强调耗时费力的过程。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The climbers began the arduous journey before dawn.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: arduous 常形容任务艰难而漫长。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scientist made a painstaking comparison of the samples.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: painstaking 强调仔细、耐心。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The doctor told him to avoid strenuous exercise.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: strenuous 多指体力或强度大。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her performance looked effortless.
antonyms.difference_note: effortless 强调看起来不费劲。

antonyms.example_sentence: The instructions made the task easy.
antonyms.difference_note: easy 是最直接反义。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The plan was elaborate but practical.
confusable_words.difference_note: elaborate 是复杂或精心设计，不一定费力。

word_family.note: 帮助理解本词的出发点。
word_family.example_sentence: The project required months of labor.
He copied the notes laboriously by hand.
The laboriousness of the task discouraged volunteers.

related_words.note: 含 labor 的“共同工作”构词联想，但意义已转为合作。
related_words.example_sentence: The researchers collaborated on the study.

source.raw_ocr_text: ia #eX vt， (根据目前趋势做出 ) HM; 预计; 估计 estimate or forecast (something) on the basis of present trends: The new building is projected to be finished in the fall. 大楼预计秋天完工 。 vt. TEE; 投射 ( 光 、 影 、 图像等 ) cause (light, shadow, or an image) to fall on a surface: The one light projected shadows on the wall. —&¥) £39 LARP BT. 派生 projection /pra'dzekfan/ n. (根据目前趋势所做的 ) 预测;推测; 估计 an estimate of future possibilities based on a current trend: computer projections of the general-election result 使用计算机对大选结果所作的预测 | a fresh projection of U.S. population by the year 2020 对 2020 年时美国人口的最新预测

headline.core_en: to estimate a future result; to send outward; a planned piece of work

familiar_bridge.summary: project 既是“项目”，也可以作动词：把未来数字“预计出来”，或把形象、光线、声音“投射出去”。

core.main: project 是多义词：考试中常见的是预计趋势、表现形象，以及投射影像或声音。

usage_groups.group_title: 根据数据预计未来结果
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于经济、人口、销售、成本等，根据现有趋势推算未来。

examples.sentence: Analysts project slower growth next year.
examples.translation: 分析师预计明年增长会放缓。
examples.teaching_note: project growth/decline 是经济和数据语境常见用法。

examples.sentence: The report projects that demand will rise sharply by 2030.
examples.translation: 报告预计到 2030 年需求将大幅上升。
examples.teaching_note: project that... 可接从句表示预测。

usage_groups.group_title: 表现出某种形象或态度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于人、机构、文本有意或无意地传达某种印象。

examples.sentence: The leader tried to project confidence during the crisis.
examples.translation: 危机期间，这位领导者试图表现出自信。
examples.teaching_note: project confidence 是自然搭配。

usage_groups.group_title: 把光、声音或影像投射出去
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于技术和物理语境，表示光线、声音或图像向外投射。

examples.sentence: The device projects the image onto a blank wall.
examples.translation: 这个设备把图像投射到一面空白墙上。
examples.teaching_note: project an image 是投影义。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 名词 project 指有目标和步骤的一项工作。

examples.sentence: The research project examines how memory changes with age.
examples.translation: 这个研究项目考察记忆如何随年龄变化。
examples.teaching_note: 名词 project 是最常见基础义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Scientists predict heavier rainfall in the region.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: predict 最普通；project 常基于数据或趋势推算。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her calm voice showed confidence.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: show 更普通；project 可带有有意表现某种形象的意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Economists forecast a modest recovery.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: forecast 常用于天气、经济等预测。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency estimated the cost at two million dollars.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: estimate 偏估算数值；project 偏推算未来趋势。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The painting conveys a sense of isolation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: convey 与 project 的“表现/传达形象”义接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report concealed the true cost of the program.
antonyms.difference_note: 在“表现/传达”义中，conceal 是隐藏不表现。

antonyms.example_sentence: Technical language obscured the main point.
antonyms.difference_note: obscure 与清楚投射或传达相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The projectile landed far from the target.
confusable_words.difference_note: projectile 是名词“抛射物”，不是 project 的普通名词“项目”。

word_family.note: project 的名词派生，可对应多个义项。
word_family.example_sentence: The budget projection was too optimistic.
Projected costs continued to rise.
The projector displayed the chart on the screen.

related_words.note: 和“向前投射/发展方向”有迁移联系。
related_words.example_sentence: The company changed its growth trajectory.
